title: EMERGENCY: stop the orphan reconciler in prod
description: The reconciler is currently republishing 100 jobs every 5 min on each scanner-worker pod, creating duplicate stream messages that workers waste cycles re-processing (confirmed by 'skipping duplicate sbom ingestion' log lines). Remove the runJobReconciler goroutine from cmd/scanner-worker/main.go. Ship this independently before the bigger refactor so prod gets relief immediately. The catalog walker + workers will drain the 1093-job backlog naturally over ~45h.
notes: Files: cmd/scanner-worker/main.go\nApproach: Removed the runJobReconciler goroutine startup; left the function body in place for the outbox refactor to delete cleanly. Confirmed via prod logs that the reconciler was claiming exactly 100 jobs per 5-min tick (batchSize cap) and workers were processing 'skipping duplicate sbom ingestion' rows — direct evidence of self-inflicted duplicate work.

title: GetArtifactVulnSummary counts duplicate purls — inconsistent with GetSBOMVulnSummary
description:
GetSBOMVulnSummary dedupes purls per SBOM via a (SELECT DISTINCT purl FROM component WHERE sbom_id...) subquery, but GetArtifactVulnSummary (db/queries/vulnerability.sql:70-83) joins raw component rows — the same purl appearing in multiple component rows of one SBOM is counted multiple times, so artifact badge counts disagree with the SBOM page for the same SBOM.

Fix: apply the same DISTINCT-purl subquery pattern in GetArtifactVulnSummary. One query edit + make generate + regression test.
acceptance_criteria: An SBOM containing the same purl in two component rows produces identical severity counts from GetArtifactVulnSummary and GetSBOMVulnSummary. Regression coverage in tests/vuln_integration_test.go. make check passes.
notes:
Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql, internal/repository/vulnerability.sql.go (regen), tests/integration_test.go, tests/vuln_integration_test.go
Approach: replaced raw component JOIN in GetArtifactVulnSummary with a (SELECT DISTINCT purl FROM component WHERE sbom_id = ...) subquery, matching the GetSBOMVulnSummary pattern. Added TestVulnDuplicatePurlSummaryParity integration test.

title: Vuln detail: resolve by canonical_id/alias and aggregate across aliased records
description:
GetVulnerabilityByID matches native id only, and the affected artifacts/components queries join pv.vulnerability_id = @id — so the GHSA record detail page misses purls mapped via the GO record (and vice versa), and there is no lookup by CVE/canonical id at all.

Fix: resolve the path param against id OR canonical_id (idx_vulnerability_canonical_id exists), then aggregate affected artifacts/components across ALL records sharing the resolved canonical_id. Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql (GetVulnerabilityByID, ListAffectedArtifactsByVuln, ListAffectedComponentsByVuln), internal/service/search_vuln.go. Depends on ocidex-0le.1 (same query file, dedup semantics).
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/vulns/CVE-xxxx resolves via canonical_id; detail page for any aliased record shows the union of affected artifacts/components across GO-/GHSA- records sharing that CVE. make check passes.
notes:
Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql, internal/repository/vulnerability.sql.go (generated), internal/service/search_vuln.go, tests/vuln_integration_test.go
Approach: Changed GetVulnerabilityByID to match id OR canonical_id (prefers exact id match via ORDER BY). Changed ListAffectedArtifactsByVuln and ListAffectedComponentsByVuln to join through vulnerability table and filter by v.canonical_id instead of pv.vulnerability_id — aggregates across all aliased records. Service layer now passes row.CanonicalID downstream. Regression test TestVulnAliasDetailResolution (port 8004) covers all three cases: native id, GHSA alias, and canonical CVE id.

title: Dedupe aliased OSV records by canonical_id in vuln counts and top-vulns list
description:
OSV returns multiple records per real-world vuln (e.g. GO-2024-xxxx + GHSA-yyyy for one CVE); both are stored and both map to the same purl. canonical_id exists (commit 683b8e0) but no query dedupes on it: ListTopVulnerabilities lists the same CVE 2-3x, and GetSBOMVulnSummary / GetArtifactVulnSummary / the stats query (db/queries/stats.sql:103-117, COUNT(DISTINCT pv.vulnerability_id)) inflate severity counts.

Fix (read-time): dedupe by canonical_id. Counts become COUNT(DISTINCT v.canonical_id); top-vulns list picks one representative record per canonical_id (DISTINCT ON (canonical_id) preferring severity != UNKNOWN, then latest modified_at). Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql, db/queries/stats.sql, then make generate.
acceptance_criteria: A purl mapped to both a GO- and GHSA- record sharing the same CVE alias yields 1 finding in SBOM/artifact summaries, 1 in home stats, and 1 row in the top-vulns list. Integration test added to tests/vuln_integration_test.go. make check passes.
notes:
Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql, db/queries/stats.sql, internal/repository/*.sql.go, tests/vuln_integration_test.go
Approach: read-time dedup via COUNT(DISTINCT v.canonical_id) in GetSBOMVulnSummary, GetArtifactVulnSummary, GetVulnStats; ListTopVulnerabilities rewritten with DISTINCT ON CTE picking best-severity representative per canonical group, then joining back all aliases for aggregate counts. Fixed pre-existing test bug: compResp["data"] → compResp["components"]. Added TestVulnAliasDedup covering all four queries.

title: OSV integration hardening (post-review)
description: Findings from an end-to-end review of the OSV integration (epic ocidex-b32 + follow-ups: alias severity resolution, canonical_id, vulnerability_reference, fixed-version matching, vuln UI). Covers correctness bugs (alias double-counting, summary inconsistencies, Wolfi ecosystem gap), robustness gaps (withdrawn records, pagination, backoff), efficiency wins, and value opportunities (CVE search). Each child is scoped for a 15-30 minute agent session. Review plan: ~/.claude/plans/we-recently-did-some-floofy-corbato.md

title: Go vulnerabilities all showing UNKNOWN severity
description: Go security database advisories (GO-YYYY-NNNN) use an empty top-level severity[] array and store the severity string in affected[].database_specific.severity. The toRow function only calls DeriveSeverity on rec.Severity (CVSS vectors), so these always fall through to UNKNOWN. Fix: parse database_specific.severity as a fallback in both Record and Affected structs.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/client.go, internal/vuln/severity.go, internal/vuln/refresh.go, internal/vuln/refresh_test.go, internal/vuln/severity_test.go
Approach: Go security database advisories ship severity in database_specific.severity (not CVSS vectors). Added DatabaseSpecific struct to both Record and Affected. Added normalizeSeverityLabel fallback in severity.go. Modified toRow to call databaseSpecificSeverity when DeriveSeverity returns UNKNOWN. Note: existing UNKNOWN records in the DB will be corrected on the next vuln refresh cycle.

title: Fix component overview bugs: LIMIT truncation, missing vuln table, flat artifact table
description: Three bugs from the new component overview flow: (1) GetComponentVersions SQL has LIMIT 200, truncating stdlib-like components with 1800+ SBOMs to 4 visible versions; (2) the version drill-down has no vulnerability table; (3) the artifact table in drill-down is flat and needs hierarchical grouping by artifact with collapsible rows (collapsed by default).
notes:
Files: db/queries/search.sql, web/src/api/queries/components.ts, web/src/pages/ComponentOverview.tsx
Approach: (1) Increased LIMIT 200 → 5000 in GetComponentVersions SQL. (2) Added enabled option to useComponentVulns hook; added vuln table to drill-down matching ComponentDetail pattern. (3) Replaced flat artifact table with hierarchical collapsible groups by artifactId, collapsed by default.

title: Vuln refresh aborts on records with no aliases (NOT NULL violation)
description: aliases TEXT[] NOT NULL: nil Go slice -> NULL -> 23502 on RHSA/DEBIAN records with no aliases. Aborts entire refresh; leaves vulnerability rows but 0 package_vulnerability. Coalesce nil->empty; make hydrate resilient (skip bad record, skip its mapping).
close_reason: Coalesce nil aliases -> empty array; hydrate skips unstorable records instead of aborting. internal/vuln/store.go + refresh.go + test.

title: CI pipelines report Succeeded on step failure (tektonic report-status never re-exits)
description: tektonic GitHubStatusReporter renders work steps with onError:continue + exit-code capture, then a final report-status step reads the code, POSTs GitHub status, but always exits 0 — so TaskRun/PipelineRun report Succeeded even when a step failed. This masked the broken v0.0.1 release (only 1 of 8 images built). Fix in pfenerty/tektonic: add failOnError option (default true) that makes report-status 'exit $exit_code'; consume via re-synth in ocidex/.tektonic. Related: ocidex-kn6.
notes:
Fix implemented in pfenerty/tektonic (PR #22, branch fix/report-status-fail-on-error): GitHubStatusReporter gains failOnError option (default true) appending 'exit $exit_code' to report-status after the POST. tektonic: npm build + 322 tests green (2 new failOnError assertions).

VERIFIED on homelab cluster (ocidex-ci): faithful reproduction of the generated pattern -> report-status exit $exit_code makes TaskRun StepFailed/False; control with legacy 'exit 0' shows Succeeded (the bug). Also synthed ocidex from the branch (npm install --no-save): all 24 report-status tasks gain the exit line; set-status-pending + git-clone correctly unaffected.

BLOCKED on human review: (1) merge tektonic PR #22 to main; (2) in ocidex/.tektonic run 'npm install @pfenerty/tektonic' to advance package-lock.json to merged commit, 'make tekton-synth', commit regenerated .tekton/tasks/*.yaml. Then Part C: merge ocidex PR #67 (digests), cut v0.0.2 -> first stable digest-pinned chart -> prod moves off :main. Related: ocidex-kn6.
close_reason: Fixed in tektonic PR #22 (failOnError, default true); consumed via re-synth in ocidex PR #68. Verified on-cluster: report-status re-exit makes TaskRun StepFailed vs legacy exit-0 Succeeded.

title: Phase 2: keyset/cursor pagination for growth lists
description: Replace OFFSET+COUNT(*) OVER() with keyset paging on /sboms,/components,/components/distinct,/artifacts,/artifacts/{id}/sboms,/artifacts/{id}/versions. New cursor helper, CursorParams/CursorMeta types, frontend infinite-query. Run make generate + make openapi.
notes: Keyset conversion completed for plain-column lists: Artifacts (name,type,id), ListSBOMsByArtifact (created_at DESC,id). Added generic encodeStringCursor/decodeStringCursor in cursor.go; ArtifactFilter/SBOMByArtifactPage carry cursor state. Added CountSBOMsByArtifact (GetArtifact no longer reads COUNT(*) OVER off the list). Aggregate-ordered lists (distinct components, licenses, artifact versions) intentionally left on OFFSET per scope decision (keyset over computed aggregate has no index benefit). Frontend: useArtifactsInfinite + load-more on Artifacts page; useArtifacts/useArtifactSBOMs single-page (offset dropped) for diff picker/version history/siblings. Verified: tests/keyset_pagination_test.go (no gaps/dupes paging size 2).
close_reason: Keyset pagination for plain-column lists + read-query caps; aggregate lists and worker queries documented as intentionally deferred/unbounded. Verified by integration tests.

title: Phase 1: efficiency indexes (graph, trigram search, purl_type)
description: New migration adding indexes: dependency(sbom_id,depends_on), component(sbom_id,bom_ref), component(sbom_id,name,group_name), pg_trgm GIN on component.name + license.name, expression index on purl_type, sbom(created_at,id). Drop redundant session.token_hash / api_key.key_hash indexes. Verify with EXPLAIN ANALYZE.
notes: Files: db/migrations/00039_efficiency_indexes.sql. Approach: CONCURRENTLY indexes for dep-graph (sbom_id,depends_on / sbom_id,bom_ref), per-SBOM list sort (sbom_id,name,group_name), pg_trgm GIN on component.name+license.name, purl_type expression index, sbom(created_at,id) for keyset. Dropped redundant session/api_key hash indexes. Verified index usage via EXPLAIN ANALYZE on 60k synthetic rows.

title: DB efficiency: scale paging, schema, and large-graph loads
description: OCIDex must load quickly as component/dependency/link tables grow. Five phases: (1) indexes on graph+search paths, (2) keyset/cursor pagination for growth-prone lists, (3) bound unbounded endpoints, (4) cache dashboard stats, (5) fix changelog N+1 + batch ingestion inserts. See plan file evaluate-the-database-efficiency-kind-firefly.md.
close_reason: DB efficiency epic complete: indexes, keyset pagination, read-query caps, stats cache, changelog N+1 fix, and CopyFrom batch ingestion. Aggregate-ordered lists and worker backfill queries documented as intentionally out of scope.

title: Provenance never found for multi-arch images: signatures live on the index, SBOMs stored per child digest
description: Scanner expands multi-arch index and stores SBOMs keyed by per-platform child digest, but cosign/Tekton Chains sign the index (tag target). Provenance enricher looks up sig/att by child digest -> 404 -> signaturePresent=false for all multi-arch images. Verified on ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-api: tag sha-3cefd317 -> index 7e4242 (.sig=200,.att=200), amd64 child 3342 (stored as SBOM) has no .sig. Fix: propagate index digest scanner->ScanRequest->sbom.index_digest->SubjectRef->provenance enricher (prefer index digest, fall back to child). Relates ADR-032.
notes: Files: db/migrations/00037_index_digest.sql, internal/scanner/{scanner,catalog,nats_submitter}.go, internal/scanner/engine/dispatcher.go, internal/service/{job,sbom,enrichjob}.go, db/queries/{scan_jobs,sbom,search,enrichment_jobs}.sql, internal/enrichment/{enrichment,provenance/provenance}.go, internal/worker/enrichment/worker.go. Approach: propagate multi-arch index digest scanner->scan_jobs->sbom.index_digest->enrich claim->SubjectRef; provenance enricher prefers IndexDigest for sig/att lookup + verification binding, falls back to child digest. Verified live: fresh scan of ocidex-api main child d886d0e0 got index_digest 02e9e8a2 (signed), provenance now signaturePresent=true/attestationPresent=true/builderId=tekton chains. Unit test TestDiscover_PrefersIndexDigest.

title: enrichment Enqueue idempotency_key collides across enrichers, dropping oci-metadata+provenance jobs
description: EnrichJobService.Enqueue set idempotency_key=sbomID for all enrichers. With the pre-existing UNIQUE(idempotency_key) constraint (migration 00034) still present after 00036 added UNIQUE(sbom_id,enricher_name), only the first enricher (user) inserts; oci-metadata and provenance hit the idempotency_key unique violation and are silently swallowed (isUniqueViolation -> nil). Net: scanned/uploaded SBOMs get user enrichment but no metadata or provenance. Fix: per-enricher idempotency key (sbomID:enricherName) in internal/service/enrichjob.go. Found while implementing ocidex-6zt. Relates ADR-033 / ocidex-1sx.
notes: Files: internal/service/enrichjob.go. Approach: idempotency_key now sbomID:enricherName instead of sbomID. The legacy UNIQUE(idempotency_key) constraint (00034) survived 00036, so a sbom-only key collided and silently dropped oci-metadata+provenance jobs. Verified e2e.

title: Wire enrichment submitter into scanner-worker so scanned SBOMs get enriched
description: Registry-scanned SBOMs are ingested in cmd/scanner-worker but the enrichment submitter (creates enrichment_jobs + enrich.hint) is only registered in the API process. The relayed sbom.ingested NATS subject has no consumer, so scanned images never get oci-metadata/provenance enrichment. Fix: register enrichment.NewNATSSubmitter in cmd/scanner-worker on its in-process bus. Relates to ADR-033 / ocidex-1sx.
notes: Files: cmd/scanner-worker/main.go. Approach: registered enrichment.NewNATSSubmitter on the scanner-worker in-process bus so scan ingests enqueue per-enricher jobs + enrich.hint (submitter previously ran only in API, which never ingests scanned SBOMs). Verified e2e: re-scan creates user/oci-metadata/provenance jobs, all succeed.

title: Migrate enrichment to per-enricher workers: compose + chart + drop 'all' enqueue
description: Compose ran the monolithic enrichment-worker; chart deployed only enrichment-worker/scanner-worker. Submitter enqueued an 'all' job plus per-enricher jobs. Complete the ADR-033 migration: docker-compose runs the 3 per-enricher workers; Helm chart deploys per-enricher worker Deployments/PodMonitors/ScaledObjects (range over enricherWorkers, shared enrichmentWorker sizing); submitter stops enqueuing 'all'. Coordinated deploy required (chart + API image together).
notes: Files: docker-compose.yml (3 per-enricher services replace enrichment-worker); internal/enrichment/submitter.go (drop 'all' from enqueue loop); charts/ocidex/templates/deployment-enricher-workers.yaml (new, ranges enricherWorkers), removed deployment-enrichment-worker.yaml; scaledobjects.yaml + podmonitors.yaml range per-enricher; values.yaml adds enricherWorkers list, enrichmentWorker kept as shared sizing. Validated: helm template/lint, go build ./..., enrichment+worker tests pass. Coordinated deploy required; homelab enrichmentWorker.replicas override still applies.

title: Operator chart: image tag falls back to appVersion (plan-5sj)
description: charts/ocidex-operator rendered image.tag directly with default 'latest' and no appVersion fallback, so prod could not resolve a semver by dropping the tag override. Fixed deployment.yaml to use '.Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion' and changed values default tag to ''. Parent epic: plan-5sj

title: CI images missing OCI labels — refactor reverted Dockerfile LABEL fix
description: The docker build refactor (2e0ad6b) reverted the LABEL-in-Dockerfile fix (35cfdfa) and went back to buildctl --output label: options, which buildkit drops for attestation/index builds. Verified live ocidex-api:main config has zero labels. Restore LABEL instructions in docker/Dockerfile + docker/web/Dockerfile and remove dead $LABELS path from build.sh/release.sh and generated task YAMLs.
notes:
Root cause: label:KEY=VALUE was passed as an image EXPORTER (--output) attribute, which buildkit silently ignores. label: only works as a dockerfile FRONTEND opt (--opt label:). Verified live + via cluster TaskRuns.

Fix (CLI-only, no Dockerfile LABELs):
- Source: .tektonic/jobs/image-build/{build.sh,release.sh,spec.ts}
  - config labels via --opt label:KEY=VALUE
  - OCI annotations via --output ...,annotation-index.KEY=VALUE (read by GHCR)
  - per-image title/description moved to IMAGE_TITLE/IMAGE_DESCRIPTION step env
  - build.sh folded in the refactor's amd64-only + DATE=$CREATED intent
- Regenerated 16 .tekton/tasks/image-{build,release}-*.k8s.yaml via ts-node pipeline.ts
Validated: ad-hoc TaskRun running verbatim generated script -> all 9 config labels AND 9 index annotations present (incl. spaced values).

title: fix(ci): HOME=/workspace causes cred-init to write .docker, blocking image builds
description: Tekton cred-init copies Docker credentials to $HOME/.docker. openapi-check and frontend-lint set HOME=$(workspaces.workspace.path) via nodeEnv in shared.ts. cred-init writes /workspace/workspace/.docker as uid 1024, but image-build steps run as uid 1000, causing buildkit context transfer to fail with 'open .docker: permission denied'. Fix: change nodeEnv HOME from workspace path to /tmp.
notes:
Files: .tektonic/shared.ts, .tekton/tasks/frontend-lint.k8s.yaml, .tekton/tasks/openapi-check.k8s.yaml
Approach: Changed nodeEnv HOME from workspace path to /tmp. Confirmed root cause via cluster investigation: openapi-check pod had HOME=/workspace/workspace, cred-init wrote .docker as uid 1024 mode 0700. Image builds run as uid 1000 and can't read it. buildkit fsutil fails before .dockerignore exclusions apply. Fix verified: test TaskRun pod shows HOME=/tmp for all containers.

title: Provenance enricher misses buildkit-native in-toto attestations
description:
The provenance enricher (internal/enrichment/provenance/provenance.go) only recognises two OCI artifact types in extractFromReferrers:
- application/vnd.dev.cosign.artifact.sig.v1+json (cosign sig)
- application/vnd.dsse.envelope.v1+json (DSSE envelope / Tekton Chains)

Buildkit's native provenance and SBOM attestations, pushed with --opt attest:provenance=mode=max and attestation-manifest-referrers=true, land in the OCI referrers index with artifact type application/vnd.in-toto+json. The enricher's switch doesn't match this type so extractFromReferrers returns found=false, the cosign tag-scheme fallback also finds nothing, and all images built by the pipeline are stored as unsigned.

Add a case for application/vnd.in-toto+json in extractFromReferrers and parse its payload as a raw in-toto statement (not DSSE-wrapped). Reuse or mirror the existing extractFromAtt logic.
notes:
Files: internal/enrichment/provenance/{provenance.go,parse.go,verify.go,provenance_test.go}
Approach: Added inTotoArtifactType constant and a third case in extractFromReferrers for application/vnd.in-toto+json. Added AttArtifactType field to RawArtifacts to track which format was discovered. Added extractFromRawInToto in parse.go that parses raw in-toto JSON directly (no DSSE envelope, no base64 decode). Updated buildProvenance to dispatch to extractFromRawInToto when AttArtifactType == inTotoArtifactType. Guarded DSSE verification block in applyVerification so raw in-toto atts are not DSSE-verified (no envelope sig to check). Added TestDiscover_RawInToto and raw_intoto table case in TestBuildProvenance.

title: Fix .dockerignore missing .docker entry
description: Tektonic CI builds fail with 'error from sender: open .docker: permission denied'. The buildkit client-side context sender walks the workspace with --local context=. and tries to stat .docker. The rootless buildkitd container cannot access it, causing every image build task to error before buildctl even runs.\n\nAdd .docker (and .github, .tektonic) to .dockerignore so they are excluded from the build context sent to buildkitd.
notes: Files: .dockerignore\nApproach: Added .docker, .github, .tektonic/ under Dev tools section to exclude them from buildkit context transfer

title: refactor: shared enrichment worker scaffolding helper
description:
The three new per-enricher cmd/ mains repeat ~180 lines of identical setup (config, DB pool, NATS, extension manager, signal handler). Extract into a reusable function so each cmd/ main is ~20 lines.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Read cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go in full. Understand the two entry paths: run() (daemon) and runEnrichOnce() (--once K8s Job mode).
2. Create internal/worker/enrichment/worker.go:
   package enrichmentworker

   type RunConfig struct {
       EnricherName string  // e.g. "provenance", "oci-metadata", "user"
       HintDurable  string  // unique NATS durable consumer, e.g. "enrich-hint-provenance"
   }

   // Run is the shared daemon entry point for all per-enricher workers.
   func Run(enrichers []enrichment.Enricher, cfg RunConfig) error { ... }

   // RunOnce enriches a single SBOM (ENRICH_SBOM_ID env var) with the given enrichers and exits.
   func RunOnce(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool, enrichers []enrichment.Enricher) error { ... }

3. Copy the body of cmd/enrichment-worker/run() and runEnrichOnce() into these functions.
   Replace hardcoded enricher registration with the enrichers []enrichment.Enricher parameter.
   Replace hardcoded HintDurable: "enrichment-hint" with cfg.HintDurable.
   Pass cfg.EnricherName to ClaimNext (the jobqueue worker needs it to filter enrichment_jobs).
   The insecureResolver and trustResolver should be built inside Run() from the registry service
   (same as cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go lines 100-102) and passed to enrichers that need them
   via a WithRegistrySvc option or built directly.
4. flox activate -- bash -c 'export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$PATH"; make build' — existing cmd/enrichment-worker must still compile.

NOTE: The jobqueue.Config.HintDurable field controls which NATS durable consumer the worker uses.
Each per-enricher worker MUST have a unique value here to avoid competing for the same consumer.
Convention: "enrich-hint-" + enricher_name.

ACCEPTANCE: make build green; internal/worker/enrichment/worker.go exists with Run() and RunOnce(); make test green.

DEPENDS ON: ocidex-atd.19
acceptance_criteria: internal/worker/enrichment/worker.go exists with Run() and RunOnce(); make build and make test green
notes:
Files: internal/worker/enrichment/worker.go (new), cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go (modified)
Approach: extracted Run() and RunOnce() from cmd/enrichment-worker into shared package enrichmentworker. Used EnricherFactory func(*pgxpool.Pool) []enrichment.Enricher instead of []enrichment.Enricher to allow resolver-dependent enrichers (oci, provenance) to be built after the pool is created inside Run(). cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go reduced to ~40 lines. RunConfig.EnricherName scopes ClaimNext; RunConfig.HintDurable sets the unique JetStream consumer per worker type.

description:
Three new per-enricher binary entry points: one per enricher, each claiming only its own enricher_name rows from enrichment_jobs.

Read cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go (lines 100-115) for the exact enricher option wiring.

title: feat: fan-out N enrichment_jobs rows per SBOM on ingest
description:
With per-enricher job rows (ocidex-atd.19), the code that creates enrichment_jobs must insert one row per enricher so each per-enricher worker has a row to claim. The NATS hint (doorbell) stays as a single publish per SBOM.

title: fix: bind provenance verification to image digest

title: feat: provenance enricher — native ECDSA verification

When the resolved registry mode is public_key: parse the PEM with crypto/x509 and verify the simplesigning signature and the DSSE PAE-encoded attestation with crypto/ecdsa. Set Provenance.Verified true or false. Mode none or no anchor leaves Verified nil (tier 1 display-only). Mode keyless leaves nil with a TODO (deferred, needs sigstore-go). No cosign/sigstore deps. TrustResolver type + WithTrustResolver option added to provenance.go. TestECDSAVerification covers valid/no-anchor/tampered cases against real cosign.pub + real fixtures.

title: Surface cosign signatures & SLSA provenance in ocidex UI
description:
Tekton Chains signs every apko-cicd/ocidex image (artifacts.oci.signer=x509, format=simplesigning), emits SLSA provenance (slsa/v2alpha4), and logs to the public Rekor log (transparency.enabled=true). All attached to images in ghcr.io/pfenerty/ as OCI referrers. ocidex currently ignores signing data.

Goal: a Provenance & Signatures card on the SBOM detail page + a signing-status badge in list views, surfacing builder, source repo+commit, build time, Rekor entry, and verification status.

Trust model (generalizable, not hardcoded): verification is per-registry configuration. Tier 1 fetch & display (always on, no anchor) records what signatures/attestations exist; badge 'Signed (unverified)'. Tier 2 verify when a registry has a PEM public-key anchor -> native ECDSA verification -> badge 'Verified'. ghcr.io/pfenerty anchor = contents of apko-cicd/cosign.pub. Keyless (Fulcio) fields reserved, impl deferred.

Full plan: ~/.claude/plans/we-just-finised-some-calm-stardust.md

title: Prod: revert SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY to 1 — Pi4 CPU was the real bottleneck
description: Sequel to ocidex-ujj.76. With concurrency=2 + 3 replicas, node talos-1q6-aue hit 92% CPU and scanner pods OOMKilled / context-deadline-exceeded on ingest. Postgres turned out to be at 137m of 1000m limit — not the bottleneck. The real cap is Pi4 cores: two concurrent Syft processes saturate the node. Revert SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY 2→1; keep 3 replicas (one scan per Pi), postgres limit bump (harmless headroom), and scanner-worker resource bumps (overprovisioned for concurrency=1 but no cost).
notes: Files: k8s/overlays/prod/patches/env-prod-scanner.yaml. Reverted SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY 2→1. Kept replicas=3 and postgres/scanner-worker resource bumps. Net concurrency vs original baseline: 2→3 (+50%) cluster-wide. Pi4 cores remain the actual cap until storage / Syft tuning / hardware addresses it.

title: Outbox queries: id-based Claim/Finish/Fail + stuck-running sweep
description: Add SQL queries that operate on scan_jobs.id (UUID) instead of nats_msg_id: ClaimScanJobByID (UPDATE WHERE id AND state='queued' RETURNING joined registry fields), FinishScanJobByID, FailOrRequeueScanJobByID (increments attempts; sets 'failed' if attempts>=max else 'queued'), RequeueStuckRunning (sweep that moves stale 'running' rows back to 'queued', failing if max retries hit). Add idx_scan_jobs_queued ON (state, created_at) WHERE state='queued' for poll-loop efficiency. Service-layer methods that wrap them. Doesn't change behavior yet — sets up the primitives for the atomic switch in the next issue.
notes: Files: db/migrations/00031_scan_jobs_outbox_indexes.sql, db/queries/scan_jobs.sql, internal/repository/repository.go, internal/service/job.go, internal/scanner/engine/dispatcher_test.go\nApproach: ClaimScanJobByID + ClaimNextQueuedJob both UPDATE...RETURNING with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Registry credentials joined into the RETURNING projection so workers need no second query. FailOrRequeueScanJobByID gates retry policy in one place (CASE on attempts vs max). RequeueStuckRunning replaces the orphan reconciler — only stuck-job sweep the outbox model needs. Partial indexes on (state='queued', created_at) and (state='running', last_attempt_at) keep both fast.

title: Atomic switch: workers + producers to outbox pattern
description: Single atomic deploy. (1) Producer (NATSSubmitter, catalog walker): publish payload becomes {"id":"<uuid>"} on subject ocidex.scan.hint. DB row still inserted in same flow but is now the only source of truth. (2) Worker: replace internal/scanner/nats_extension.go's handleMsg with a hint handler that calls processJob(ctx, id). processJob: ClaimScanJobByID → if no row, ack and skip; else Syft scan; on success FinishScanJobByID; on failure FailOrRequeueScanJobByID. (3) Add DB poll loop in scanner-worker running every 30s, calling processJob for each queued row up to maxConc. (4) Consumer config: AckPolicy=AckExplicit (ack on receive), MaxDeliver=1, MaxAckPending small (just buffering). NATS no longer does retry. (5) Replace TimeoutJobs with RequeueStuckRunning in the existing reaper goroutine. (6) Delete: runJobReconciler, NATSSubmitter.Republish, routeToDLQ subject routing, scannerMaxDeliver constant, SCANNER_MAX_ACK_PENDING + ENRICHMENT_MAX_ACK_PENDING config, reconcile_attempts column (down migration). Also handles enrichment-worker analogously.
notes: Files: ~18 files. New: internal/scanner/nats_hint.go, db/migrations/00032_drop_reconcile_attempts.sql. Deleted: internal/scanner/nats_extension.go, internal/scanner/engine/extension.go, tests/nats_scanner_dedup_test.go. Refactored: Dispatcher (pure scan+ingest, returns sbomID), NATSSubmitter (publishes {id} hint only), scanner-worker main (NATSHintExtension + RequeueStuckRunning sweep, no reconciler). Net -702 lines. Verified end-to-end via TestScanToEnrichFlow with the new path.

title: Outbox refactor: Postgres-as-queue, NATS-as-doorbell
description: Replace the dual-write (NATS stream + scan_jobs DB) with the transactional outbox pattern. scan_jobs.state becomes the single source of truth for queued work; NATS carries only {id} hints for fast wakeup. Workers also poll the DB as a fallback. This removes the orphan reconciler, NATS-side retry/DLQ, MaxAckPending coordination, and the same-Msg-Id republish dance — all of which exist to paper over the dual-write problem. Diagnosed root cause of the prod 1093-queued / 574-failed pile-up: reconciler creating duplicate stream messages because slow-but-not-orphaned jobs cross the 10-min threshold under Pi4 throughput limits.
notes: All 6 children closed. Epic complete. Outbox refactor verified end-to-end via TestScanToEnrichFlow. Net change across the 6 commits: large deletion-heavy refactor (~1200 lines net deleted). The dual-write antipattern, orphan reconciler, NATS-side retry/DLQ, MaxAckPending coordination, and Nats-Msg-Id dedup window are all gone. Production rollout: emergency fix .74.1 already on main (a7b8425 → 2e0d619 series); the outbox-refactor branch carries .74.2 through .74.6 as separate logical commits, ready to merge to main.

title: fix(web): Caddyfile try_files ordering breaks /auth/* proxy

title: Ship k8s/overlays/prod as Flux OCI artifact from CI
description:
## Why

Using a mutable :main image tag with imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent causes nodes to cache stale images, making deploys non-deterministic and rollbacks ambiguous. The fix is to publish the prod kustomization as an OCI artifact from CI with pinned commit-SHA image tags baked in, and have the homelab OCIRepository watch it instead of a GitRepository.

## What

### In ocidex CI (GitHub Actions):
1. After building images, tag them with the commit SHA (ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-{api,scanner-worker,enrichment-worker,web}:$SHA) in addition to :main
2. Run 'kustomize edit set image' on k8s/overlays/prod to replace :main tags with :$SHA
3. Push k8s/overlays/prod as an OCI artifact: oci://ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-manifests:$SHA, also tag :main
4. Use 'flux push artifact' + 'flux tag artifact'

### In homelab repo:
1. Replace the ocidex GitRepository (source for ocidex-app Kustomization) with an OCIRepository pointing to oci://ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-manifests:main
2. Add a GHCR image pull secret if needed (or use provider: github)
3. Update the ocidex-app Kustomization to reference the OCIRepository source

## Acceptance
- git push to ocidex main triggers CI that pushes both app images and an OCI manifest artifact, all tagged with the commit SHA
- Homelab Flux picks up the new artifact within 1 minute and rolls the deployment
- kubectl get pods shows the new image SHA on running pods
- Rollback = pushing a previous artifact SHA tag; no git revert needed
close_reason: CI manifests job pins SHA tags via kustomize edit set image and pushes k8s/ as OCI artifact; homelab swapped to OCIRepository source

title: docker/Dockerfile migrate stage COPY uses wrong GOPATH; image has never built
description:
The migrate stage at docker/Dockerfile:43 does:

  COPY --from=builder /root/go/bin/goose /goose

But the upstream golang:1.25-bookworm image sets GOPATH=/go (not $HOME/go). 'go install github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose@v3.27.0' in the builder stage writes the binary to /go/bin/goose, so the COPY fails with:

  failed to compute cache key: failed to calculate checksum of ref ...:
    "/root/go/bin/goose": not found

The image has never published to GHCR — that's why ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-migrate appears not to exist. (Earlier hypothesis that the package was private was wrong; GHCR returns the same 403 for missing packages as for private ones.)

Fix: change line 43 to

  COPY --from=builder /go/bin/goose /goose

The cache mounts at lines 11 and 14 ('--mount=type=cache,target=/root/go/pkg/mod' and '--mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build') also point at the wrong path (real module cache is /go/pkg/mod, real build cache is /root/.cache/go-build which is right by accident). They're dead weight — caches that never hit. Not breaking, but worth fixing as a follow-up for build time.

Blocks ocidex-0my.14 (smoke deploy).
notes:
Files: docker/Dockerfile (lines 11,14).
Resolution: the original COPY bug (migrate stage copying /root/go/bin/goose) is moot — ocidex-ujj.59 deleted the migrate stage and goose install entirely, so the image now builds. The only live remnant was the two BuildKit module-cache mounts targeting /root/go/pkg/mod; the golang:1.25-bookworm image sets GOPATH=/go, so 'go mod download' writes to /go/pkg/mod and those mounts never hit. Changed both to /go/pkg/mod. Left --mount target=/root/.cache/go-build as-is (correct: GOCACHE defaults to $HOME/.cache/go-build, HOME=/root). Build-time verification (cache populates, faster rebuilds) is on a docker host; docker is unavailable in this env.
close_reason: COPY bug obsolete (migrate stage removed in ujj.59); fixed remaining wrong module-cache mount paths /root/go/pkg/mod -> /go/pkg/mod in docker/Dockerfile.

title: NATS StatefulSet missing server_name; JetStream cluster cannot start
description:
In a 3-replica JetStream cluster, nats-server requires each pod to have a unique server_name. The base manifest at k8s/base/nats-statefulset.yaml (args: -js, -cluster, -routes) does not set --name, so pods crashloop with:

  nats-server: jetstream cluster requires server_name to be set

Observed on rpi-cluster after first prod deploy (ocidex-0my.11). nats-0 and nats-1 both in CrashLoopBackOff.

Fix: add downward-API POD_NAME env and pass --name $(POD_NAME) to nats-server args. Standard pattern:

  env:
    - name: POD_NAME
      valueFrom:
        fieldRef:
          fieldPath: metadata.name
  args:
    - --name
    - $(POD_NAME)
    - -js
    - -cluster
    - nats://0.0.0.0:6222
    - -routes
    - nats://nats-0.nats:6222,nats://nats-1.nats:6222,nats://nats-2.nats:6222

Also worth checking whether the embedded-mode (single replica) overlays need different args.

Blocks ocidex-0my.14 (smoke deploy).
notes:
Files: k8s/base/nats-statefulset.yaml.
Approach: added downward-API POD_NAME env (fieldRef metadata.name) and prepended '--name $(POD_NAME)' to the nats-server args so each pod in the 3-replica cluster gets a unique server_name; K8s expands $(POD_NAME) in args because it is a defined env var in the same container. Base-only change — prod overlay uses base directly (where the crashloop occurred); dev overlay replaces args with [-js] (single node) and is unaffected (verified via kubectl kustomize: prod shows --name $(POD_NAME); dev still replicas=1, args -js). Cluster confirmation on rpi-cluster (nats-0/1/2 Ready, no 'requires server_name' log) is the deploy-time step.
close_reason: Added --name $(POD_NAME) + downward-API POD_NAME env to k8s/base/nats-statefulset.yaml; kustomize renders verified for prod (clustered) and dev (single node).

title: Release pipeline: git-cliff, semver tags, OCI annotations, ldflags version
description: Add a real release flow on top of the dev-channel GHCR builds. Manual tag-driven (vX.Y.Z) releases produce immutable images with full org.opencontainers.image.* annotations (consumed by ocidex's own scanner), a CHANGELOG.md generated by git-cliff, and a GitHub Release. Go binaries embed version/commit/date via -ldflags so 'ocidex --version' and the OCI annotation agree. Folds the OCI-annotation/ldflags additions to .github/workflows/images.yml that were originally scoped to ocidex-0my.1. See plan: /home/patrick/.claude/plans/examine-the-beads-backlog-dynamic-pascal.md

Files added:
- internal/version/version.go (Version/Commit/Date vars set via -ldflags)
- cliff.toml (git-cliff config: conventional commits, keep-a-changelog template, skips chore(release|beads|deps))

Verified:

title: 1.1.E.1 — Smoke deploy + verify ocidex.app end-to-end
description:
Capstone. After all prerequisite 0my.* issues are done:

1. Push images via 0my.A1 workflow; capture commit SHA for image tag.
2. Pin SHA in k8s/overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml (or in the homelab flux-kustomization).
3. Commit + push the homelab repo; watch 'flux get kustomizations' until ocidex Ready.
4. 'kubectl -n ocidex get pods' — all of api(2), web(1), scanner-worker(1), enrichment-worker(1), nats(1-3), postgres(1) Running/Ready. migrate Job Completed.
5. 'curl -fsSL https://ocidex.app/health' → 200; '/ready' → 200.
6. Browser: load https://ocidex.app/, click 'Sign in with GitHub', complete OAuth round-trip, land on authed home page.
7. Add a public registry (e.g., docker.io/library), trigger a scan of one small image (e.g., docker.io/library/alpine), confirm an SBOM ingests and surfaces in the UI.
8. File any rough edges as new beads issues; do NOT silently fix them in this capstone.

title: 1.1.D.3 — Cloudflare DNS for ocidex.app
description:
Verify how the cluster's Cloudflare Gateway resolves new hostnames. ocidex.app is in the same Cloudflare account as pfenerty.com so the existing tunnel + gateway apply unchanged. Two possibilities:
  (a) The cloudflared tunnel + Cloudflare Gateway API controller auto-manages DNS for any hostname listed in an HTTPRoute attached to cloudflare-gateway (zero-touch).
  (b) The DNS CNAME / tunnel hostname must be added manually in the Cloudflare dashboard.

Check by inspecting flux/apps/podinfo (does podinfo.pfenerty.com 'just work' or was it manually configured?) and the cloudflare-gateway/install.yaml controller config. If (b), add the CNAME for the apex ocidex.app pointing at the tunnel via Cloudflare dashboard (and verify proxy/orange-cloud setting matches what podinfo uses).
close_reason: Cloudflare gateway controller (pl4nty/cloudflare-kubernetes-gateway) auto-manages DNS from HTTPRoute.hostnames. Verified by absence of any manual DNS records for podinfo.pfenerty.com / oci.pfenerty.com in the homelab repo. No CNAME work required for ocidex.app — listing the hostname in the HTTPRoute (added in ocidex-0my.11) is sufficient.

title: 1.1.D.1 — Flux app: homelab/talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/
description:
Create the new Flux app directory in the homelab repo, modeled on flux/apps/podinfo/ and flux/apps/zot/:

Files:
  - namespace.yaml: Namespace 'ocidex'
  - kustomization.yaml: aggregates the files below
  - flux-kustomization.yaml: kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1 Kustomization that points at GitRepository ref of pfenerty/ocidex repo, path=k8s/overlays/prod, targetNamespace=ocidex, prune=true, interval=10m. Add the GitRepository source separately (or reuse if one exists for ocidex CI).
  - httproute.yaml: HTTPRoute 'ocidex' in namespace ocidex, parentRef cloudflare-gateway/cloudflare-gateway, hostnames=[ocidex.app], rules: prefix '/api' → ocidex-api:80, default '/' → ocidex-web:80

Wire into flux/apps.kustomization.yaml as a new entry. Reference patterns: flux/apps/podinfo/route.yaml (hostname/parentRef shape) and flux/apps/zot/helmrelease.yaml.values.httproute (multi-rule structure).
notes:
Files (in pfenerty/homelab, commits 5d4e460 + fix 6c9cb48):
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/namespace.yaml — Namespace 'ocidex'
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/git.source.yaml — GitRepository 'ocidex-prod' in flux-system, https://github.com/pfenerty/ocidex.git @ main, 5m. Named '-prod' to avoid collision with the existing 'ocidex' GitRepository in ocidex-ci ns (tracks 'tektonic' branch for Tekton).
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/httproute.yaml — HTTPRoute attached to cloudflare-gateway/cloudflare-gateway, hostname ocidex.app, /api → ocidex-api:80, / → ocidex-web:80.
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/flux-kustomization.yaml — INNER Flux Kustomization 'ocidex-app' reconciling ./k8s/overlays/prod from sourceRef ocidex-prod into the ocidex namespace.
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/kustomization.yaml — local aggregator: namespace + git.source + flux-kustomization + httproute. (.12 will add secrets.yaml.)
- talos-cluster/flux/ocidex.kustomization.yaml — OUTER Flux Kustomization 'ocidex' reconciling ./talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex from sourceRef flux-system; dependsOn cilium + cloudflare-gateway; SOPS decryption wired for .12's secret.
- talos-cluster/flux/kustomization.yaml — registers ocidex.kustomization.yaml.

Approach: two-layer reconciliation matching the podinfo pattern. Root flux-system Kustomization → applies the outer ocidex Flux Kustomization → which applies apps/ocidex/ contents (namespace, ocidex-prod GitRepo, HTTPRoute, inner Flux Kustomization) → which applies ocidex repo's k8s/overlays/prod into the ocidex namespace.

Gotcha (fix 6c9cb48): initial commit (5d4e460) had a chicken-and-egg bug — the root Flux Kustomization pointed directly at the ocidex repo's k8s/overlays/prod with sourceRef=ocidex-prod, but the ocidex-prod GitRepository was defined under apps/ocidex/ which nothing reconciled. Sources for an external repo must be applied by an outer Kustomization sourcing the homelab repo itself before any inner Kustomization can use them — same pattern as podinfo's path=./talos-cluster/flux/apps/podinfo with sourceRef=flux-system.

title: 1.1.B.3 — goose-migrate Job in k8s/base
description:
Add k8s/base/migrate-job.yaml: a Kubernetes Job that runs 'goose -dir db/migrations postgres "$DATABASE_URL" up' against the in-cluster Postgres before the API starts serving.

Approach options (decide during implementation):
  (a) Use the API image with command override — but the API image is distroless/static and doesn't have goose. Either bake goose into a dedicated migrate image (extra Docker target), OR
  (b) Add a 'migrate' target to docker/Dockerfile that copies the goose binary + db/migrations/ into a distroless image (cleanest). Run goose binary directly.

Option (b) recommended. Job's restartPolicy: OnFailure, backoffLimit: 3. envFrom: secretRef ocidex-secrets so DATABASE_URL is available.

Idempotency: Flux re-applies on each reconcile but a completed Job is immutable. Use a generated name (kustomize secretGenerator-style hash) or include a timestamp annotation that forces recreation when migrations change. Simplest: bump a label / use generateName during overlay rendering when migrations change. Investigate flux's 'force' annotation behavior here.
notes:
Files: docker/Dockerfile (new migrate stage + goose install in builder), k8s/base/migrate-job.yaml (new), k8s/base/kustomization.yaml, .github/workflows/images.yml (matrix entry), Tiltfile (build + k8s_resource).
Approach: New 'migrate' Dockerfile target — distroless static image with the goose v3.27.0 CLI binary and db/migrations/ baked in. ENTRYPOINT is the goose CLI prefix; Job supplies DATABASE_URL + 'up' via args (k8s $(VAR) expansion from envFrom: ocidex-secrets). Flux force annotation handles Job immutability across re-applies. backoffLimit=5 with restartPolicy=OnFailure covers Postgres-not-ready on first deploy. Startup migrations in cmd/ocidex/main.go kept as idempotent safety net (per planning decision). Verified with kubectl kustomize on the dev overlay; docker build deferred to CI (no docker available locally).

title: 1.1.B.2 — Add Postgres StatefulSet + Service + PVC to k8s/base
description:
Dev overlay's k8s/overlays/dev/postgres.yaml uses emptyDir — fine for Tilt, not for prod. Add a production-shaped Postgres to k8s/base (or as a kustomize component so dev can opt out and keep its emptyDir variant):

  - k8s/base/postgres-statefulset.yaml: StatefulSet 'postgres', image docker.io/postgres:15.4-alpine, replicas=1, volumeClaimTemplate 'data' (1–10Gi, storageClassName: local-path), env POSTGRES_USER/DB from configMap, POSTGRES_PASSWORD via secretRef 'ocidex-secrets', PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
  - k8s/base/postgres-service.yaml: ClusterIP 'postgres', port 5432
  - Wire into k8s/base/kustomization.yaml

Use storageClassName 'local-path' (local-path-provisioner is in the cluster). Resources: requests cpu 100m / memory 256Mi, limits cpu 500m / memory 1Gi (the Pi nodes have 4–8GB; postgres on ARM does fine within these bounds).

If a kustomize component is cleaner, place under k8s/components/postgres/ and have dev opt out, prod opt in — but a plain base resource with a dev patch that swaps to emptyDir is simpler. Decide during implementation.
notes:
Files: k8s/base/postgres-statefulset.yaml, k8s/base/postgres-service.yaml, k8s/base/kustomization.yaml, k8s/overlays/dev/patches/postgres.yaml, k8s/overlays/dev/kustomization.yaml, Tiltfile. Deleted k8s/overlays/dev/postgres.yaml.
Approach: NATS-style base+dev-patch. Base StatefulSet uses volumeClaimTemplates with storageClassName=local-path (5Gi), POSTGRES_PASSWORD via secretKeyRef from ocidex-secrets. Dev patch adds emptyDir volume and removes volumeClaimTemplates; Tiltfile injects POSTGRES_PASSWORD=devpass into the generated secret. title: 1.1.A.1 — GitHub Actions multi-arch image builds to GHCR
description:
Add .github/workflows/images.yml that, on push to main, uses docker/setup-qemu-action + docker/setup-buildx-action + docker/build-push-action to build linux/amd64 + linux/arm64 images and push to GHCR. Four images, all from existing Dockerfiles:

  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-api               (docker/Dockerfile target=api)
  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-scanner-worker    (docker/Dockerfile target=scanner-worker)
  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-enrichment-worker (docker/Dockerfile target=enrichment-worker)
  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-web               (docker/web/Dockerfile)

Tags: 'sha-<short>' AND 'main'. Auth via GITHUB_TOKEN + permissions: packages: write. No release tagging yet — that comes later.

This is the unblock for the Pi cluster pulling real images. Talos is arm64, so arm64 must be in the matrix from day one.
notes:
Files: .github/workflows/images.yml
Approach: matrix over 4 targets (api, scanner-worker, enrichment-worker, web) on ubuntu-latest. docker/setup-qemu-action@v3 + docker/setup-buildx-action@v3 + docker/build-push-action@v6, platforms linux/amd64,linux/arm64, tags via docker/metadata-action@v5 ('main' branch tag + 'sha-<short>'). GHA cache scoped per image. Concurrency group images-<ref> with cancel-in-progress=false so a half-pushed manifest list does not happen. Login to ghcr.io via GITHUB_TOKEN, permissions.packages: write at workflow level.
Follow-up (human): after first successful run, flip the 4 GHCR packages to Public via the GitHub web UI so Talos can pull without imagePullSecrets. Relevant for issues .11 (Flux app) and .14 (smoke deploy).

title: Migration 00026 hangs on production-size component table
description: The first version of migration 00026 (UNKNOWN main-module backfill) ran regexp_replace per row across the full component table. With 874k rows it never finished — production startup hung 20+ min between 'database connected' and the HTTP server starting, so /auth/login returned 502 Bad Gateway from nginx. Fix: drive the UPDATE from the small main_module CTE inside a DO loop, use the existing idx_component_sbom_id index, and replace regex match with LIKE prefixes. Also restrict to type IN ('library','application') so the 785k file rows (legitimately NULL version) are skipped. Manually verified on the live DB: full migration runs in ~5s instead of >20m.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs in seconds on a 1M-row component table. ocidex-app starts cleanly, /auth/login returns 307 to GitHub OAuth instead of 502.
notes: Files: db/migrations/00026_backfill_unknown_main_module_version.sql (rewritten as DO loop), tests/main_module_backfill_test.go (replay updated to match). Root cause: original migration ran regexp_replace on every row of component (874k rows in prod), which the planner couldn't push down past the WHERE clause. Fix drives the UPDATE from the small main_module CTE inside a DO loop (one UPDATE per SBOM with a source label), uses idx_component_sbom_id, and matches by LIKE prefix instead of regex. Filters version IN ('UNKNOWN','') and type IN ('library','application') to skip the 785k file rows whose NULL version is legitimate. Verified on the live DB: full migration runs in ~5s instead of >20m. ocidex-app starts cleanly post-migration; /auth/login returns 307 to GitHub OAuth as expected.

title: Strip distro version from identity qualifier
description: normalizeComponentPurl keeps full distro=alpine-3.14.3 string in identity key; alpine-3.15.0 hashes differently → all apk packages report as remove+add on alpine version bumps. ~14-18 phantom pairs per dex/alpine release. Same for fedora rpm. ADR-0019 example only covered stable distro=wolfi-os; never tested paired version drift. TDD: paired test cases in TestNormalizeComponentPurl + new TestDiffComponents alpine-version-bump case fail first; then strip trailing -<version> suffix from distro qualifier value. Amend ADR-0019 Rule 1 table.
acceptance_criteria: alpine-3.14.3 and alpine-3.15.0 normalize to same identity key. dex v2.30.3 → v2.31.0 diff: alpine-baselayout, alpine-keys, busybox, libssl1.1, musl, apk-tools, etc. appear once with direction=upgraded.
notes: Files: internal/service/changelog.go (normalizeComponentPurl, distroVersionSuffixRe), internal/service/changelog_test.go (5 new TestNormalizeComponentPurl cases + TestDiffComponents_DistroVersionDrift + TestDiffComponents_DistroFamiliesStayDistinct), docs/adr/0019-diff-identity-model.md (Rule 1 table + worked example). Approach: distro qualifier value is normalized to family by stripping a trailing -<version> suffix (regex -[0-9][A-Za-z0-9.-]*$). alpine-3.14.3 and alpine-3.15.0 both collapse to 'alpine'; family separation preserved by purl namespace. Two pre-existing TestNormalizeComponentPurl cases updated to reflect new contract.

title: Frontend tree drops disconnected roots (keycloak)
description: DiffTreeView.tsx builds allRefs from edge set only; nodes in roots[] with zero in-edges and zero out-edges silently disappear. Confirmed: keycloak 8.4 → 8.5 returns 20 keycloak-* roots from /api/v1/sboms/diff-tree, only the 2 with outgoing edges render. TDD: add DiffTreeView.test.tsx with disconnected-root case first, then build nodes from props.tree.nodes (server-authoritative). Drop nameMap multi-key indirection — use change.nodeRef for change→node lookup.
acceptance_criteria: Test web/src/components/DiffTreeView.test.tsx exercises disconnected-root case and passes. All 20 keycloak-* nodes render in browser tree view for 8.4 → 8.5 diff.
notes: Files: web/src/components/DiffTreeView.tsx, web/src/components/DiffTreeView.test.tsx (new). Approach: dropped the nameMap multi-key indirection and built the TreeNode map directly from props.tree.nodes keyed on bomRef. Edges feed children only; tree membership no longer requires edge presence. Changes join to nodes via change.nodeRef (backend-populated per ADR-0021 §B3). Tests cover disconnected-root rendering, mixed connected/disconnected roots, and the empty-tree fallback.

title: B6: Add isDirect to ComponentSummary in graph + diff responses
description: Direct = reachable in 1 hop from the synthetic root (metadata.component.bom-ref). Compute server-side; expose on ComponentSummary used by DependencyGraph and DiffTree. Enables show-transitive toggle (D5) and direct-default tree view (G3).
notes: Files: internal/service/changelog.go\nApproach: directSet built from outgoing edges of synthetic root in computeRootsAndDirect(). ComponentSummary.IsDirect set from directSet[bomRef] in buildNodes().

title: B5: Compute tree roots server-side (anchor on metadata.component.bom-ref)
description: Today the frontend picks roots as nodes with zero in-edges, with a 'first 10' fallback. Move to backend. If metadata.component.bom-ref is in the dependency graph, its outgoing edges define the canonical roots (the image's direct dependencies). Otherwise compute zero-in-edge roots and surface them; never silently fall back.

title: ADR: Image flavor detection from SBOM contents
description: Document layered flavor detection at ingest time: (1) OS metadata properties (syft:image:os:name etc.), (2) purl-type fingerprint, (3) tag suffix fallback. Document apk-ambiguity resolution via ?distro qualifier, distroless handling, and known limitations (multi-distro images, tools that omit OS metadata). Output: docs/adr/020-image-flavor-axis.md.
notes:
Files: docs/adr/0020-image-flavor-axis.md
Approach: Layered SBOM-content detection at ingest time, persisted on sbom.flavor (text, indexed). Layer 1 = OS metadata properties (syft:image:os:name, version-id; Trivy equivalents; CycloneDX 1.5+ structured fields) — most precise, distinguishes alpine-3.18 vs alpine-3.19. Layer 2 = dominant purl-type fingerprint (apk/deb/rpm) with ?distro qualifier disambiguation for the apk-family ambiguity (alpine vs wolfi vs chainguard). Layer 3 = tag-suffix heuristic from a hardcoded suffix list (last resort). Schema is single lowercased string of form <family> or <family>-<version_id>; sentinel 'unknown' for unresolved (non-NULL so flavor stays a non-nullable grouping axis). Manual override deferred to a future IngestParams.Flavor field. Tag-suffix parsing rejected as primary because conventions vary too much across codebases; manual config rejected for backfill cost.

title: ADR: Backend-computed diff tree and rollups
description: Document the principle that DiffTree responses are pre-computed on the backend: roots, isDirect flags, descendantChanges aggregates, nodeRef cross-references, ComponentDiff.direction. Frontend renders only. Output: docs/adr/021-backend-computed-diff-tree.md.
notes:
Files: docs/adr/0021-backend-computed-diff-tree.md
Approach: Enrich the existing DiffTree response in place (rejected: separate /diff-tree/structured endpoint, status quo). Six normative additions: (1) roots[] computed from metadata.component.bom-ref's outgoing edges, falling back to zero-in-edge nodes, never to 'first 10' guess; (2) isDirect on each ComponentSummary (1-hop from synthetic root); (3) direction on each ComponentDiff (added/removed/upgraded/downgraded/modified) using a deb-aware comparator — replaces frontend classifyChange/debVersionCompare; (4) nodeRef on each ComponentDiff for O(1) join to graph nodes — replaces fragile frontend name/purl/bom-ref maps; (5) descendantChanges aggregate per node from a single DFS at the tree root — drives rollup chips; (6) ChangeSummary expanded to include upgraded+downgraded. Cycle handling normative (visit-once per ancestor). Wire growth ~2% on a typical 500-component SBOM. Backwards-compatible (additive) with B-then-C ordering enforced via beads deps so frontend never depends on a missing field.

title: ADR: Diff identity model (purl-base + qualifiers)
description: Document how a component's identity is computed for diff matching. Cover: purl-without-version when present, qualifier inclusion (?distro, ?arch, ?repository_url), fallback (type, group, name) tuple, name-suffix normalization rule (regex -[0-9][0-9.]*$), survivor-guard for the reconcile pass. Output: docs/adr/019-diff-identity-model.md.
notes:
Files: docs/adr/0019-diff-identity-model.md
Approach: Layered identity model with three rules — (1) primary key = purl-base + identity-bearing qualifiers (distro, arch, epoch, repository_url) with alphabetical ordering and an explicit allowlist, noise qualifiers like download_url/checksum dropped; (2) fallback = (type, group, name) tuple when no purl; (3) versioned-name post-pass with a symmetric survivor guard so multi-version installs (gcc-11+gcc-12 → gcc-12+gcc-13) don't false-collapse. ADR documents normative rules, lays out the qualifier policy as a partition table, and references downstream issues bqh.25/26/27 (E1/E2/E3) for implementation + tests. CLAUDE.md ADR-summary table update is deferred to bqh.38 (H2).

title: Diff & tree display: backend-computed, flavor-aware
description:
Overhaul of SBOM diff + dependency-tree UX. Design principles:
1. Tree is the default; flat is the audit view; both stay one click apart.
2. Header summaries must reconcile with what is visible — per-ancestor rollups in tree.
3. Files are not packages — exclude server-side everywhere.
4. Backend does the heavy lifting; UI just renders.
5. Flavor (alpine/debian/wolfi/distroless/...) is a first-class timeline axis derived from SBOM contents at ingest, not from tag parsing.
6. No silent fallbacks — empty states are explicit and offer the flat view.

Phases:
A. Correctness fixes (file noise in changelog, badge palette, unsafe root fallback, empty-tree handling).
B. Backend diff enrichment — direction, descendantChanges, nodeRef, roots, isDirect, expanded ChangeSummary.
C. Frontend simplification — drop debVersionCompare/classifyChange/adjacency build, render directly from B.
D. Tree controls — centralized expansion state, expand-all-changed, show-context toggle, show-transitive toggle, localStorage prefs.
E. Identity tightening — survivor-guard for versioned-name reconcile, purl qualifiers in key.
F. Flavor axis — sbom.flavor column, ingest-time detection (OS props + purl fingerprint + tag fallback), changelog grouping, UI tabs.
G. Direct vs transitive — anchor tree on metadata.component.bom-ref, isDirect flag, direct-default view.
H. Docs + ADRs.

See conversation transcript and ocidex-0ji (tree perf spike) for context.
close_reason: All 38 issues complete. Backend-computed diff tree, flavor axis, identity tightening, tree controls, and docs all shipped.

title: Artifact "SBOMs" tab is a Versions tab — paginate, label, and count by version
description:
## Problem

The "SBOMs" tab on the artifact detail page mislabels and miscounts what it shows, and its pagination is broken. Surfaced by the Traefik artifact, where the footer says "Showing 1-25 of 39" but only ~4 rows are visible.

These are two faces of the same problem: the tab is conceptually a Versions tab, but it is wired to a SBOM-paginated API and labelled with the raw SBOM count.

## Evidence

- API GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/sboms (internal/api/search.go:282) returns paginated raw SBOM rows. Query ListSBOMsByArtifact in db/queries/artifact.sql:50-69 uses COUNT(*) OVER() over raw SBOMs and ORDER BY s.created_at DESC LIMIT/OFFSET.
- Frontend SBOMsTab (web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx:21-24) calls groupSBOMsByVersionAndArch (web/src/utils/groupSBOMs.ts:8-27), keeping only the newest SBOM per (subject_version, architecture). For Traefik, 39 raw SBOMs collapse to 4 unique versions.
- Pagination (SBOMsTab.tsx:262-265) reflects the raw count (39), not the displayed row count.
- Paging "next" advances by raw-SBOM offset; older revisions of the same 4 versions can be returned, again collapsing — pagination does not actually reach distinct content.
- Tab button (ArtifactDetail/index.tsx:227-234) reads "SBOMs ({a().sbomCount})" — SBOM-flavored label and count for a version-flavored table.

## Root cause

Vocabulary drift. The page was built around versions but kept SBOM nomenclature and a SBOM-paginated API.

## Fix direction

Introduce GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/versions that: paginates by subject_version, rolls up architectures and newest revision per version server-side, returns a version count. Frontend: rename tab to Versions, switch to new endpoint, drop client-side grouping.

## Affected files

- internal/api/search.go
- internal/service/search_sbom.go
- db/queries/artifact.sql
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/index.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx
- web/src/utils/groupSBOMs.ts
- web/src/api/queries/artifacts.ts
notes:
Files: db/queries/artifact.sql, internal/repository/artifact.sql.go (generated), internal/repository/repository.go, internal/service/search.go, internal/service/search_artifact.go, internal/api/types.go, internal/api/search.go, internal/api/router.go, web/src/api/client.ts, web/src/api/queries/artifacts.ts, web/src/api/queries/index.ts, web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/VersionsTab.tsx (new), web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/index.tsx
Approach: Added GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/versions with two-CTE SQL query (sboms_meta + DISTINCT ON for newest-per-version + array_agg for architectures). CountArtifactVersions feeds versionCount into ArtifactDetail. Frontend: replaced SBOMsTab with VersionsTab (server-side grouping, no client-side groupSBOMsByVersionAndArch), tab renamed to Versions (N). Deleted SBOMsTab.tsx and groupSBOMs.ts.

title: Skip re-hydrating unchanged vuln records using querybatch modified timestamps
description:
batchResult (internal/vuln/client.go) parses each vuln modified timestamp but queryChunk discards it; hydrate then performs GetVuln + upsert + reference-replace for every unique ID on every cycle, serially. For a changed ecosystem like npm that is thousands of sequential GETs per 6h cycle for records that have not changed.

Fix: propagate (id, modified) pairs from QueryPurls; in hydrate, skip GetVuln/upsert/ref-replace when the stored modified_at is >= the reported modified (bulk lookup of stored modified_at values, e.g. a GetVulnerabilityModifiedAts query over ids). Keep mapping replacement as-is since fixed_version derivation needs the record — for skipped records reuse stored data or keep existing mapping rows. Files: internal/vuln/client.go, refresh.go, db/queries/vulnerability.sql.
acceptance_criteria: A refresh where all records are unchanged performs zero /v1/vulns/ GETs (asserted via the fake in refresh_test.go) while purl mappings remain correct. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/client.go, refresh.go, store.go, db/queries/vulnerability.sql, internal/repository/vulnerability.sql.go
Approach: QueryPurls now returns map[string][]QueryRef (ID+Modified) instead of map[string][]string. hydrate partitions IDs via bulk GetVulnerabilityModifiedAts lookup into toFetch (new/changed, still calls OSV GetVuln) vs toLoad (unchanged, reconstructed from stored raw JSON via GetVulnerabilitiesRaw). Verified: full test suite + race detector pass, TestHydrateSkipsUnchangedRecords asserts zero GetVuln calls on an all-unchanged cycle with correct fixed-version mappings preserved. No sqlc drift.
close_reason: Verified pre-existing uncommitted implementation: build, full test suite (race), and sqlc generate all clean; committed as 5ab4a47

title: Scheduled incremental refresh never covers new purls in unchanged ecosystems
description:
Incremental mode (selectChangedPurls) only queries purls whose ecosystem CSV advanced. A purl first seen via SBOM ingest is covered only by the ingest-time extension; if that lookup fails (2-minute timeout, network blip) or INGEST_VULN_LOOKUP_ENABLED=false, the purl stays unknown until its ecosystem CSV happens to advance — potentially days for quiet ecosystems.

Fix: in selectChangedPurls (internal/vuln/refresh.go), union in purls that have no package_vulnerability rows (add a global ListUnknownComponentPurls query modeled on ListUnknownSBOMComponentPurls in db/queries/vulnerability.sql). Note: purls with zero vulns also have no rows, so they get re-queried each cycle — acceptable until a known-clean state exists (see the purl_vuln_state backlog item).
acceptance_criteria: With all ecosystem CSVs unchanged, a purl absent from package_vulnerability is still included in the scheduled refresh query set. Unit test in refresh_test.go. make check passes.
notes:
Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql, internal/repository/vulnerability.sql.go (generated), internal/vuln/refresh.go, internal/vuln/store.go, internal/vuln/refresh_test.go
Approach: Added ListUnknownComponentPurls SQL query (global version of ListUnknownSBOMComponentPurls); wired it through Store interface and PGStore; selectChangedPurls now always unions in unknown purls before the early-return check. Extracted mergePurls helper to keep cyclomatic complexity at 15. New test TestSelectChangedPurls_IncludesUnknownPurls verifies a purl with no mappings is included when no ecosystem CSV changes.

title: Withdrawn OSV records are stored and displayed
description:
Record.Withdrawn (internal/vuln/client.go:35) is parsed but never checked anywhere. Withdrawn records returned by querybatch keep purl mappings and remain in the vulnerability table indefinitely, showing up in counts and lists.

Fix: in hydrate (internal/vuln/refresh.go), treat a non-empty withdrawn as a tombstone: delete the vulnerability row (FK cascades clean up package_vulnerability and vulnerability_reference) or at minimum skip it and exclude it from the returned records map so replaceMappings drops its mappings. Test with a withdrawn fixture in refresh_test.go.
acceptance_criteria: A record with withdrawn set produces no vulnerability row and no purl mappings after refresh; previously-stored withdrawn records are removed on next refresh. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/refresh.go (hydrate guard), internal/vuln/store.go (PGStore.DeleteVulnerabilityByID), db/queries/vulnerability.sql (DeleteVulnerabilityByID query), internal/repository/vulnerability.sql.go (generated), internal/vuln/refresh_test.go (two new tests)
Approach: Added withdrawn check in hydrate after GetVuln; calls DeleteVulnerabilityByID (FK cascades) then continues past the record so it is excluded from the records map and thus from replaceMappings refs. Zero new lint issues.

title: fixed_version can come from the wrong package or release branch
description:
fixedVersionForPurl / firstFixedVersion (internal/vuln/refresh.go:435-517) scan ALL affected[] entries of a record without matching affected.package against the queried purl. Multi-package advisories (common for GHSA monorepos) and per-release Debian/Alpine affected entries can yield another package or branch fixed version. Non-SEMVER (ECOSYSTEM) ranges always fall through to firstFixedVersion, compounding this.

Fix: filter affected entries to those whose package purl (base, version-stripped) matches the queried purl before range matching; when no confident match exists, store an empty fixed_version rather than a possibly-wrong one. Add table-driven tests including a multi-package record. ECOSYSTEM-range version comparison is out of scope (follow-up).

title: Incremental refresh: apk purls gated on Alpine CSV only — Wolfi/Chainguard advisories missed
description:
purlTypeToEcosystem (internal/vuln/ecosystem.go) maps apk -> Alpine, but apk purls carry the distro in the namespace (pkg:apk/wolfi/..., pkg:apk/chainguard/... — OSV ecosystems Wolfi and Chainguard). A new Wolfi advisory does not bump Alpine modified_id.csv, so incremental refresh never re-queries Wolfi purls. Same class: deb -> Debian only (Ubuntu missed). Directly relevant: this deployment images are Wolfi-based (apko-cicd).

Fix: make the mapping return multiple candidate ecosystems per purl type (apk -> Alpine+Wolfi+Chainguard; deb -> Debian+Ubuntu); a type counts as changed if ANY of its CSVs advanced; keep per-ecosystem state rows as today. While there, add missing single mappings (cran -> CRAN, conan -> ConanCenter, bitnami -> Bitnami) — unknown types currently always refresh so those are efficiency only. Files: internal/vuln/ecosystem.go, internal/vuln/refresh.go (selectChangedPurls), tests.
acceptance_criteria: With Alpine CSV unchanged and Wolfi CSV advanced, apk purls are included in the refresh set; ecosystem state rows updated per ecosystem. Unit tests cover multi-ecosystem types. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/ecosystem.go, internal/vuln/refresh.go, internal/vuln/refresh_test.go
Approach: Changed purlTypeToEcosystem from map[string]string to map[string][]string. selectChangedPurls now iterates all ecosystems per type and sets anyChanged if any CSV advanced. Added Wolfi+Chainguard to apk, Ubuntu to deb, and missing cran/conan/bitnami mappings. PurlTypeToOSVEcosystem renamed to PurlTypeToOSVEcosystems. Two new tests verify apk included when Wolfi advances and excluded when all unchanged.

title: BUG: ArtifactDetail signingStatus always empty string
description:
The GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} endpoint returns signingStatus: "" (empty string) for all artifacts, even those where the list endpoint (GET /api/v1/artifacts) correctly returns a non-empty aggregated value (e.g. "verified" or "unsigned").

Surface: The new Signing field added to the ArtifactDetail About card (ocidex-hfi.8) always shows 'Unsigned' regardless of actual signing status, because it reads from ArtifactDetail.signingStatus which is always empty.

Evidence: curl /api/v1/artifacts returns signingStatus=verified for fbba00cd (ocidex-api), but curl /api/v1/artifacts/fbba00cd returns signingStatus="". All versions in VersionsTab correctly show 'Verified' because they read from ArtifactVersionSummary.signingStatus (a separate field populated differently).

Fix: The artifact detail handler (or its service layer) needs to aggregate signingStatus across the artifact's SBOMs/versions, the same way the list handler does. Check internal/service/ and internal/repository/ for the query that backs GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id} vs GET /api/v1/artifacts and align them.

notes:

Approach: release charts pin images by digest. helm-release fetches crane, resolves each pushed image's index digest, bakes into chart values before helm package; set -e aborts before push if any digest missing (verified locally: aborts on missing scanner-worker for v0.0.1). Precedence tag>digest>appVersion keeps dev rolling :main.
DISCOVERED: no tag has all 8 images (v0.0.1 only has api; the 3 enricher-workers never built even on :main). So no stable chart ever published -> prod frozen on old :main install. Cutting v0.0.2 will be first full-8-image release; enricher-worker build success is unverified (Dockerfile targets exist). Homelab prod manifests already correct (>=0.0.0, no tag).

title: fix(ci): go-test cache redundant restore + never-updated archive
description:
go-test runs after go-build on the same shared workspace PVC. Its restore step deletes and re-extracts from the cached archive (which go-build saved with build-only deps), then go-test downloads test-only deps. The save step skips because the archive already exists — so the complete cache is never persisted.

Fix:
1. restore step: skip extraction if .go-mod already exists (seeded by go-build)
2. save step: remove the 'exists, skipping' guard so go-test always overwrites with the complete (build + test) cache
notes:
Files: .tektonic/shared.ts, .tektonic/jobs/go-test/spec.ts, .tekton/tasks/go-test.k8s.yaml, .tektonic/package-lock.json
Approach: Added goCacheTest = {...goCache, forceSave: true, skipRestoreIfPathsExist: true} in shared.ts. go-test/spec.ts now imports goCacheTest. skipRestoreIfPathsExist was added to tektonic library (pvc-backend.ts, gcs-backend.ts, task.ts, task.test.ts — commit 81df6b2). make tekton-synth regenerates go-test.k8s.yaml correctly; manual YAML patches no longer needed.
close_reason: go-test cache now skips restore when go-build has seeded workspace, force-saves test deps. Implemented in tektonic library and consumed in ocidex.

title: feat: Dockerfile stages for per-enricher worker images
description:
Each per-enricher worker needs its own Docker image for independent K8s Deployment. The existing multi-stage docker/Dockerfile pattern makes this straightforward: add a go build line per binary in the builder stage and a runtime stage per binary.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Read docker/Dockerfile in full. Note the builder stage (lines ~23-26) and existing runtime stages (e.g. AS enrichment-worker around line 39-42).
2. In the builder stage, add after the existing go build lines:
     CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build -ldflags "${LDFLAGS}" -o /app/bin/oci-metadata-worker ./cmd/oci-metadata-worker && \
     CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build -ldflags "${LDFLAGS}" -o /app/bin/user-enricher-worker ./cmd/user-enricher-worker && \
     CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build -ldflags "${LDFLAGS}" -o /app/bin/provenance-worker ./cmd/provenance-worker
3. Add three new runtime stages after the enrichment-worker stage:
   # ---- oci-metadata-worker
   FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12:nonroot AS oci-metadata-worker
   COPY --from=builder /app/bin/oci-metadata-worker /oci-metadata-worker
   ENTRYPOINT ["/oci-metadata-worker"]

   # ---- user-enricher-worker
   FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12:nonroot AS user-enricher-worker
   COPY --from=builder /app/bin/user-enricher-worker /user-enricher-worker
   ENTRYPOINT ["/user-enricher-worker"]

   # ---- provenance-worker
   FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12:nonroot AS provenance-worker
   COPY --from=builder /app/bin/provenance-worker /provenance-worker

4. Docker is not available locally; verify syntax by comparing the new stages against the existing enrichment-worker stage — they should be structurally identical.
5. flox activate -- bash -c 'export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$PATH"; make build' — Go binary build still green.

title: Build per-project Flox dev image via GitHub Actions (flox containerize)
description:
Produce a dev-workspace OCI image from the project Flox env so it can run as the dev-spaces/devbox workspace (replacing the linuxserver sshd placeholder).

Done so far:
- Added openssh, direnv, git to .flox/env/manifest.toml; set systems=[aarch64-linux, x86_64-linux].
- Drafted .github/workflows/dev-image.yml (arm64 runner ubuntu-24.04-arm, flox/install-flox-action, flox containerize -> GHCR ocidex-dev). NOT committed pending verification.

Open items:
- Verify flox containerize CLI/output for installed flox version (docker runtime vs tarball).
- Validate the containerized image can run sshd: needs host keys, login user, authorized_keys, sshd_config, entrypoint. flox containerize gives no init/sshd scaffolding. Zed gets the env via direnv so login-shell activation isn't required, but sshd must run.
- Re-lock manifest for aarch64-linux/x86_64-linux (flox activate) and confirm all pkgs resolve on arm64.
- Confirm ubuntu-24.04-arm runner availability (repo public=free, private=billed).
- Decide DRY composition: shared dev-remote env on FloxHub included by each project, vs direct packages per project (current approach).
notes: PIVOTED away from flox containerize. Its Nix fake-nss /etc symlinks fail Talos containerd at mount time (path escapes from parent); Flox's runtime activation overlay owns /etc and drops privileges, defeating in-image deref + breaking chown. New: standalone reusable project ~/code/devbox/ = Debian base + Flox + git + direnv + sshd + baked non-root patrick user (real /etc, containerd-native), plus a Helm chart (chart/) for workspace + Tailscale sidecar (serve TCP 22->127.0.0.1:2222, no node ports) and a GHA arm64 build. ocidex dev-image.yml + the openssh/direnv added to ocidex .flox are superseded. Next: build ghcr.io/pfenerty/devbox then deploy via chart / Flux HelmRelease.
close_reason: Done — reusable devbox image+chart deployed. Standalone ~/code/devbox project (Debian base + Flox + git + direnv + sshd, non-root 'dev' user), published as ghcr.io/pfenerty/devbox (image) + ghcr.io/pfenerty/charts/devbox (OCI Helm chart). Deployed in homelab dev-spaces via Flux OCIRepository+HelmRelease (chart 0.2.0), Tailscale sidecar (serve TCP 22->127.0.0.1:2222, no node ports). Three root causes solved: (1) flox-containerize Nix fake-nss symlinks rejected by containerd -> Debian base; (2) chown EPERM from NFS root_squash default SC -> local-path; (3) pubkey denied for key-only account under UsePAM no -> UsePAM yes. SSH dev@devbox.<tailnet>.ts.net works.

title: 1.0.B.18 — Make migrate an ocidex subcommand; stop auto-migrating at startup
description:
The API currently runs goose.Up at startup. In multi-replica prod that's a race + a version-skew footgun (old binary may not understand new schema after rollback). Switching to an explicit 'ocidex migrate' subcommand keeps the dev convenience of a single binary while making schema changes deliberate, and lets us delete the separate migrate Dockerfile target.

Files: cmd/ocidex/main.go, docker/Dockerfile, k8s/base/migrate-job.yaml (+ overlays), Makefile, docs/DEPLOYMENT.md, CLAUDE.md.

Acceptance:
- cmd/ocidex dispatches on argv: bare invocation runs API server (no migration); 'ocidex migrate up|down|status' runs goose
- API server startup no longer calls runMigrations
- docker/Dockerfile 'migrate' target removed; no goose install step in builder
- k8s migrate Job uses the API image with command ['/ocidex', 'migrate', 'up']
- Makefile migrate-up still works
- Docs updated to reflect non-auto-migrate behavior
