title: dev-up doesn't stop conflicting docker-compose containers
description: The legacy docker-compose dev flow (make run / embedded mode) binds host ports 5432, 4222, 5000, 8080, 3000 — all of which Tilt also port-forwards from the cluster. Because postgres in docker-compose.yml has a restart policy, it gets auto-started on Docker daemon start and silently blocks Tilt's postgres port-forward with 'address already in use'. dev-up should stop the docker-compose stack as a preflight.
notes:
Files: Makefile (dev-up target)
Approach: Added a preflight to dev-up that detects a running docker-compose stack ([ -n "$(docker compose ps -q)" ]) and runs 'docker compose stop' before starting Tilt. The two flows share host ports (5432, 4222, 5000, 8080, 3000) so they can't coexist; postgres in particular has a restart policy that brings it back on Docker daemon start, silently re-binding 5432.

title: dev-up/dev-down don't handle stale tilt processes
description: make dev-up fails with 'address already in use' when a tilt process is already running — either a suspended foreground job from Ctrl-Z or an orphaned daemon from a prior session. make dev-down only runs 'tilt down' (which removes cluster resources) and never terminates the long-running tilt process, so it accumulates. Both targets should detect and clean up stale tilt processes.
notes:
Files: Makefile (dev-up, dev-down targets)
Approach: Added a preflight to dev-up that detects existing tilt processes (pgrep -x tilt) and stops them with SIGTERM, falling back to SIGKILL after 5s. dev-down now runs tilt down first to clean up cluster resources, then kills the long-running tilt process the same way (previously dev-down only ran tilt down, leaving the daemon orphaned). Handles both stale-from-prior-session daemons and suspended foreground jobs (Ctrl-Z).

title: dev-cluster-down leaves stale talos/kube contexts
description: make dev-cluster-down removes Docker resources but not entries in ~/.talos/config or ~/.kube/config. On subsequent dev-cluster-up, talosctl renames the new context (ocidex-dev → ocidex-dev-2), so the Makefile's hardcoded --context ocidex-dev step targets a dead endpoint and fails.
notes:
Files: Makefile (dev-cluster-down target)
Approach: Added shell loops after the docker cleanup that strip stale ocidex-dev[-N] entries from talosctl config and kubectl config (contexts, clusters, users). Awk extracts the context name from talosctl config contexts handling the '*' current-context column. Also manually pruned existing stale entries and renamed the live ocidex-dev-2 (talos) / admin@ocidex-dev-3 (kube) contexts back to canonical names so the current cluster keeps working.

title: docker/api/Dockerfile only ships /ocidex; worker binaries are missing
description:
docker/api/Dockerfile builds cmd/ocidex, cmd/scanner-worker, and cmd/enrichment-worker in the build stage but only copies /ocidex into the distroless runtime stage. The K8s worker Deployments use 'args: ["/scanner-worker"]' / '["/enrichment-worker"]' which the container then passes as an arg to the ENTRYPOINT /usr/local/bin/ocidex. Result: 'workers' silently run the API binary on port 8080 — they appear Ready, never consume from NATS, and look like dead workers in any distributed-mode test.

Fix: either (a) COPY all three binaries into the runtime image and remove the hard-coded ENTRYPOINT in favour of CMD that the manifest's args replaces, or (b) consolidate to a single binary that dispatches on the first positional argument. (a) keeps the deployments correct as written.

Surfaced during ocidex-pww (local Talos + Tilt dev loop) end-to-end bring-up.
close_reason: Fixed via multi-target Dockerfile (docker/Dockerfile) and per-service image builds: ocidex-api, ocidex-scanner-worker, ocidex-enrichment-worker. Each image ships only its own binary; deployments reference the matching image. Verified end-to-end: scan jobs now move queued → running → succeeded.

title: Local Talos + Tilt dev cluster for tight inner-loop K8s development
description: Add a local-cluster dev workflow so we can deploy ocidex to Kubernetes, edit code, and see changes redeployed without round-tripping through an external registry. Uses talosctl-provisioned Talos cluster (parity with homelab), a host-side Docker registry mirror, and a Tiltfile to orchestrate build/push/deploy on save. Adds Postgres + dev secrets to the existing k8s/overlays/dev kustomization so 'make dev-up' brings up the entire stack.
notes:
Files:
- Tiltfile (root)
- tilt/talos-cluster.yaml — Talos config patch for localhost:5005 registry mirror at 10.5.0.1
- k8s/overlays/dev/postgres.yaml — Postgres Deployment+Service (emptyDir, dev creds)
- k8s/overlays/dev/kustomization.yaml — adds postgres + secretGenerator for ocidex-secrets
- Makefile — dev-registry, dev-cluster-up, dev-cluster-down, dev-up, dev-down
- CLAUDE.md — Key Commands + 'Local K8s dev loop' section

Approach:
- Talos provisioned via 'talosctl cluster create' (Docker provider, x86_64). Registry mirror points pods at localhost:5005 via host bridge IP 10.5.0.1.
- Local registry is plain registry:2 in a Docker container; Tilt's default_registry pushes to it.
- Tilt builds the single ocidex image (api + scanner-worker + enrichment-worker share the binary), kustomize applies dev overlay.
- Frontend stays as Vite local_resource (HMR is far better than rebuild loops); only Go services run in cluster.
- Postgres is a 1-replica Deployment with emptyDir; API auto-runs goose migrations on startup so no init container needed.
- Secrets via kustomize secretGenerator (disableNameSuffixHash=true so ocidex-secrets stays the literal name the base manifests reference). OAuth+session-secret stubbed for dev.

Verified:
- 'kubectl kustomize k8s/overlays/dev' renders cleanly with the new Secret + Postgres resources.
- API auto-runs migrations (cmd/ocidex/main.go:304-309), so cold-start against fresh Postgres works.

User must install Tilt before 'make dev-up' (talosctl + docker already installed).

title: Jobs tab: error detail collapses on every data refresh
description:
In Admin > Jobs, clicking 'View error' on a failed job opens a <details> element showing the error text. However, useListScanJobs has refetchInterval: 2500, which triggers a full table re-render every 2.5 seconds. Because <details> is an uncontrolled element in Solid, each re-render resets it to its default closed state, collapsing the expanded error immediately.

**Root cause:**
The <details> element in the Last Error column (Admin.tsx:944–950) is uncontrolled HTML. Solid's reconciliation replaces the DOM node on each query result update, losing the open attribute.

**Fix:**
Replace the uncontrolled <details> with a controlled expand/collapse using createSignal per job row. Track a Set or Map of expanded job IDs in the JobsTab component, and use a Show + button pattern instead of <details>/<summary>.

Example approach:
- In JobsTab, add: const [expandedErrors, setExpandedErrors] = createSignal(new Set<string>())
- In the row: replace <details> with a button that toggles the job id in the set, and a Show component that renders the error text when the id is present
- Use untrack or a stable keyed accessor so the signal survives the query update

**Affected file:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — JobsTab, the 'Last Error' column cell (lines ~943–951)
close_reason: replaced uncontrolled <details> with Set<string> signal in JobsTab scope; expanded state survives query refetches

title: Architecture switcher shows duplicates for Windows multi-arch SBOMs
description:
## Problem

When clicking into a Windows multi-arch artifact version (/sboms/{id}), the architecture switcher shows duplicate buttons (e.g., "amd64", "amd64", "amd64", "arm64"). Same problem appears in the Architectures column of the artifact detail Versions table.

## Evidence

- Arch switcher (web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx:276-290) renders one button per sibling SBOM with the same subjectVersion. No dedup on architecture.
- Scanner ociExpandIndex (internal/scanner/catalog.go:360-380) parses each manifest in an OCI image index but stores only arch. Windows multi-arch indexes contain multiple manifests sharing architecture: amd64 but differing in os.version.
- ociGetImageMetadata (internal/scanner/catalog.go:391-470) reads only config.Architecture — same problem.
- Result: multiple SBOMs per Windows variant; identical architecture enrichment; UI renders each as a separate "amd64" button.

## Root cause

Scanner and enrichment discard os and os.version. No qualifier capable of distinguishing Windows variants.

## Fix direction (two layers)

1. Scanner/enrichment: capture os and os.version alongside architecture. Option (a): extend enrichment record with explicit os/os.version fields, update SBOM list query. Option (b): composite platform string (e.g., windows/amd64/10.0.17763) when os == windows.
2. Frontend defense: dedupe arch siblings by platform identifier in SBOMDetail/index.tsx:276-290 and SBOMsTab.tsx:178-194.

## Affected files

- internal/scanner/catalog.go
- internal/enrichment/ (oci-metadata enricher)
- db/queries/artifact.sql (if option 1a)
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx
notes: Files: internal/enrichment/oci/oci.go, web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx\nApproach: Two-layer fix. (1) Enricher: added platformArchitecture() helper that builds composite 'windows/amd64/10.0.17763' string when OS=windows and os.version is present, using cfg.OSVersion from go-containerregistry v1.ConfigFile. Stored in enrichment JSONB architecture field; SQL queries unchanged. (2) Frontend: archSiblings() now deduplicates by architecture using a Set — handles existing SBOMs re-enriched before this fix and any edge cases.

title: Add per-message timeout to ProcessOne in NATS extensions
description: Add a per-message processing timeout to the ProcessOne method in internal/extension/ NATS handlers. Prevents a single slow message from blocking the consumer indefinitely.\n\nAffected: internal/extension/nats.go or similar NATS extension consumer.
notes: Files: internal/scanner/nats_extension.go, internal/enrichment/nats_extension.go, cmd/scanner-worker/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go, tests/nats_integration_test.go, tests/scan_to_enrich_test.go\nApproach: Added msgTimeout time.Duration field to both NATSExtension structs. title: Vulnerability id/alias search: resolve GHSA/CVE/OSV id not stored as vulnerability.id
description: GetVulnerabilityByID (db/queries/vulnerability.sql:192-198) matches only id = $1 OR canonical_id = $1 — it never checks the aliases TEXT[] column, so an alias id that isn't itself a stored vulnerability.id (e.g. a GHSA id that only appears inside another record's aliases array) resolves to nothing. Separately, there's no way to reach a vulnerability by typing/pasting an id anywhere in the UI. NOTE: no sitewide/global search exists in OCIDex (confirmed against db/queries/search.sql and web/src) — building one is out of scope for this hardening epic. This issue is narrowly about vulnerability id/alias lookup, matching the epic review's actual finding that CVE search is the most likely vuln workflow. Fix: (1) extend GetVulnerabilityByID's WHERE clause to include OR $1 = ANY(aliases); (2) add a small id-search box to the Vulnerabilities list page that navigates to /vulnerabilities/{id} on submit (VulnerabilityDetail already resolves id/canonical_id and shows EmptyState on a miss).
acceptance_criteria: Pasting a CVE id, GHSA id, native OSV id, or an alias-only id (one that only appears inside another vulnerability record's aliases array, not as its own id) into the Vulnerabilities page search box navigates to a working detail page. make check and make frontend-lint pass.
notes:
Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql, internal/repository/vulnerability.sql.go (regenerated), web/src/pages/Vulnerabilities.tsx, tests/vuln_integration_test.go
Approach: Re-scoped from false 'add vuln arm to global search' premise (no such feature exists in OCIDex) to the epic's actual finding: vulnerability id/alias lookup. Added OR $1 = ANY(aliases) to GetVulnerabilityByID's WHERE clause; added a search box to Vulnerabilities.tsx that navigates to /vulnerabilities/{id} (existing detail page already renders EmptyState on miss). Extended TestVulnAliasDetailResolution with a pure alias-only case (SNYK-2024-0001, never its own id/canonical_id) to cover the exact gap the fix closes -- confirmed via git stash that the 64 pre-existing golangci-lint issues are unrelated repo debt (identical count with changes stashed out); make test and go build pass; integration test compiles but skips locally (no Docker in this environment), will run in CI.

Files: db/queries/vulnerability.sql, internal/service/search_vuln.go, web/src/pages/VulnerabilityDetail.tsx, make generate + make openapi.

title: SEMVER event matching assumes sorted events; last_affected unsupported
description:
matchedFixed (internal/vuln/refresh.go) walks range events in document order with an inRange flag, but the OSV schema does not guarantee event ordering — verify against the spec and sort defensively. Additionally the Event struct (internal/vuln/client.go) has only Introduced/Fixed/Limit; the last_affected event type is not parsed at all, so introduced+last_affected intervals are treated as open-ended.

Fix: add LastAffected to Event; sort events by normalized version before interval matching; treat introduced+last_affected intervals as having no fixed version (empty string). Files: internal/vuln/client.go, refresh.go, table-driven tests with unsorted and last_affected fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: Unsorted event lists and last_affected ranges produce correct fixed-version results in table-driven tests. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/client.go, internal/vuln/refresh.go, internal/vuln/refresh_test.go
Approach: split events into introduced/upper boundaries, sort each independently by normalized semver, pair positionally; added matched bool return to matchedFixed to distinguish no-match from matched-but-no-fixed-version so last_affected intervals aren't papered over by a later range's fixed version

title: purlVersion mishandles qualifiers and percent-encoding
description:
purlVersion (internal/vuln/refresh.go) takes everything after the last @, so pkg:deb/debian/curl@1.2.3?arch=amd64 yields 1.2.3?arch=amd64, and percent-encoded versions (e.g. %2B for +) stay encoded — semver parsing fails and fixed-version matching silently degrades to firstFixedVersion.

Fix: strip ?... and #... suffixes and URL-decode the version before returning. Table-driven tests covering qualifiers, subpath, and percent-encoded versions.
acceptance_criteria: Versions with qualifiers/subpaths/percent-encoding parse and match SEMVER ranges. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/refresh.go, internal/vuln/refresh_test.go
Approach: strip ?/# suffix from purl version segment, percent-decode via url.PathUnescape before semver parsing. Added TestPurlVersion table test and a qualifiers regression case in TestFixedVersionForPurl.

title: OSV querybatch pagination (next_page_token) is ignored
description:
queryChunk (internal/vuln/client.go) parses NextPageToken per result but never follows it. Packages with more vulns than one page (kernel/chromium-class packages reached via deb/apk purls) get silently truncated mappings; the QueryPurls doc comment claims 1000-per-query is sufficient, which does not hold for those packages.

Fix: follow per-query pagination per OSV docs — re-issue the batch query with page_token set for the purls whose result returned a token, merging IDs until no tokens remain (with a sane page cap). Test with a paginated fake server in client_test.go.
acceptance_criteria: A purl whose fake response spans two pages returns the union of vuln IDs. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/client.go, internal/vuln/client_test.go
Approach: queryChunk now loops over rounds, re-batching only purls still pending a next_page_token (verified against OSV's real /v1/querybatch spec: page_token is a per-query-item field on the same endpoint). Capped at maxQueryPages=20 to guard against a runaway token loop. Added TestQueryPurlsFollowsPagination (union of two pages) and TestQueryPurlsPaginationExceedsPageCap. Existing tests pass unchanged.

title: Persist chosen CVSS vector column instead of re-deriving from raw at read time
description:
GetVulnerabilityByID (db/queries/vulnerability.sql) extracts cvss_vector from raw->severity preferring CVSS_V4 then V3 by type, while DeriveSeverity picks the HIGHEST score across entries — so the displayed vector can disagree with the stored score/severity. Worse: alias-resolved records (Go advisories, resolveAliasSeverity) get severity entries copied onto the in-memory record but raw is unchanged, so their detail page shows a score with no vector and no attack-profile chips.

Fix: add a cvss_vector TEXT column to vulnerability; have DeriveSeverity/toRow return the winning vector (including the alias-resolved one) and persist it; drop the jsonb extraction from GetVulnerabilityByID. Migration + internal/vuln/severity.go + refresh.go + queries + make generate.
acceptance_criteria: Stored cvss_vector always corresponds to the entry that produced cvss_score/severity; Go advisories with alias-resolved severity show vector + chips on the detail page. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/severity.go, refresh.go, refresh_test.go, severity_test.go, store.go; internal/service/search_vuln.go; db/migrations/00044_vulnerability_cvss_vector.sql, db/queries/vulnerability.sql, internal/repository/{models,vulnerability.sql}.go (sqlc-regenerated)
Approach: DeriveSeverity now returns the winning entry's vector alongside label/score. Threaded through Row.CVSSVector and PGStore.UpsertVulnerability. GetVulnerabilityByID now selects the plain cvss_vector column instead of re-deriving from raw JSONB via jsonb_array_elements, which disagreed with DeriveSeverity's highest-score selection rule and returned nothing for alias-resolved GO- records. No API/frontend changes needed (VulnDetail.CvssVector already existed and tolerated empty). Migration not applied/tested against a live DB this session (no local Postgres available) — verify on next DB-available session.
close_reason: Persisted cvss_vector column; DeriveSeverity returns winning vector; GetVulnerabilityByID simplified to plain column select. Verified via go build, gofmt, and full test suite (all pass); lint pre-existing failures unrelated (confirmed via git stash diff).

title: Vuln scheduler retries failed refresh every 60s with no backoff
description:
Scheduler.runIfDue (internal/vuln/scheduler.go): when Refresh fails, MarkRefreshed is not called, so the refresh stays due and re-runs on every 1-minute tick — hammering OSV for the duration of an outage.

Fix: track the last attempt time in the Scheduler and apply a retry floor / exponential backoff on failure (e.g. 5m doubling to a 30m cap), resetting on success. Add a unit test with a failing RefreshService fake.
acceptance_criteria: After a failed refresh the next attempt waits at least the backoff floor, not one tick; success resets backoff. make check passes.
notes:
Files: internal/vuln/scheduler.go, internal/vuln/scheduler_test.go, internal/vuln/refresh_test.go (fakeOSV.err/queryCalls knobs)
Approach: track lastAttempt/backoff fields in-memory on Scheduler, gated after due() check in runIfDue. Failure sets/doubles backoff (5m floor, 30m cap); success resets to 0. due()/store.LastRefreshedAt semantics unchanged. Added scheduler_test.go covering suppression, doubling, capping, and reset-on-success.

title: docs: ADR-0032 provenance verification + ADR-0033 per-enricher services
description:
Two significant design decisions shipped without ADRs: the provenance verification trust model (ocidex-atd) and the per-enricher service split (ocidex-atd.19-23). Project convention (CLAUDE.md) requires ADRs as normative contracts.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Read docs/adr/0026-pluggable-enricher.md AND docs/adr/0024-outbox-pattern-for-scan-queue.md to match the existing ADR format exactly.
2. Read internal/enrichment/provenance/verify.go, parse.go, provenance.go, and db/migrations/00036_per_enricher_jobs.sql to ensure accuracy.

Create docs/adr/0032-provenance-verification.md:
- Status: Accepted (2026-06-27)
- Context: Tekton Chains signs every apko-cicd/ocidex image (simplesigning + SLSA DSSE). OCIDex needed to surface and verify these signatures without the cosign/sigstore SDK.
- Decision: Tiered trust model per registry: none (Tier 0/1 display-only), public_key (Tier 2 ECDSA verify), keyless (reserved for future sigstore-go). Four signing-status values: unsigned / signed / verified / verification_failed. Discovery: remote.Referrers() (OCI 1.1) first, then cosign tag scheme sha256-HEX.sig/.att fallback. Verification: crypto/ecdsa + crypto/x509 only. Digest binding is required: simplesigning critical.image.docker-manifest-digest AND at least one DSSE in-toto subject digest must equal ref.Digest — prevents signature transplant. An asserted-but-unparseable attestation forces verified=false (not skip). verified=false renders as verification_failed (badge-danger), distinct from unsigned or signed.
- Consequences: No keyless/Fulcio yet. Rekor UUID not fetched (linked via logIndex). Trust resolver cached 30s per BuildInsecureHostLookup pattern. Enrichers are compile-time, not plugins (see ADR-033).
- References: internal/enrichment/provenance/, ADR-026

Create docs/adr/0033-per-enricher-services.md:
- Status: Accepted (2026-06-27)
- Context: Monolithic enrichment-worker compiled all enrichers into one binary. Provenance enricher (OCI API calls) needs independent scaling and fault isolation from oci-metadata enricher.
- Decision: Per-enricher cmd/<name>-worker/ binaries and Docker images. enrichment_jobs gains enricher_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'all' + UNIQUE(sbom_id, enricher_name). Job creation fans out N rows per SBOM (knownEnrichers). Each worker has unique NATS durable consumer ("enrich-hint-<name>") and claims only WHERE enricher_name = ?. Common scaffolding in internal/worker/enrichment/worker.go (Run/RunOnce). Adding a new enricher: new package + cmd/ binary + Dockerfile stage + tektonic imageSpecs + knownEnrichers entry.
- Consequences: Old enrichment-worker (enricher_name='all') kept during transition; remove after. NATS: shared hint subject, per-enricher durable consumers.
- References: ADR-024, ADR-026, db/migrations/00036_per_enricher_jobs.sql

Also update the ADR table in CLAUDE.md to add rows 032 and 033.

ACCEPTANCE: Both ADR files exist with accurate content matching the code; CLAUDE.md ADR table updated; make check green (no code changes).

DEPENDS ON: ocidex-atd.24

title: Clean up BuildInsecureResolver / BuildCredentialResolver in service/registry.go
description:
The two 'Build*Resolver' functions in internal/service/registry.go (lines 107-155) have both naming and implementation issues.

## Naming problems

- 'InsecureResolver' reads like 'a resolver that is insecure' or 'security-aware resolver'. It actually answers: 'is this host configured to be reached over plain HTTP / with TLS verification disabled?'
- Both functions return bare `func(...) ...` closures rather than named types, so call sites lose any semantic hint.

Proposed renames (open to discussion):
- `BuildInsecureResolver` → `BuildInsecureHostLookup` returning a named type `HostInsecurityChecker func(host string) bool`
- `BuildCredentialResolver` → `BuildCredentialLookup` returning a named type `HostCredentialLookup func(host string) (username, token string)`

## Implementation problems

1. **Re-queries every registry on every call.** Each invocation calls `svc.List(ctx, {IsAdmin: true})`, which hits the DB. These resolvers fire on every OCI pull — needs a cache (with invalidation when registries change, likely via the event bus) or at least a short-TTL memoization.
2. **Inline URL parsing duplicated.** Both functions reinvent the same 'strip scheme + trim trailing slash' logic. Extract a helper like `registryHost(r Registry) string`.
3. **`context.Background()` hardcoded inside the closure.** Discards caller context — no cancellation, no deadline, no tracing. The closure signature should accept a `context.Context`.

## Acceptance

- Resolver names make their purpose obvious without reading the body
- Named function types instead of bare `func(...) ...`
- Shared URL-normalization helper (single source of truth)
- Closures accept `context.Context`
- Some form of caching so OCI hot paths don't hit the DB per call
- Existing tests in internal/service/registry_test.go updated; `make check` passes
notes: Files: internal/service/registry.go, internal/enrichment/oci/oci.go, internal/enrichment/ocivalidate/validator.go, cmd/ocidex/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go\nApproach: Renamed BuildInsecureResolver→BuildInsecureHostLookup and BuildCredentialResolver→BuildCredentialLookup, returning named types HostInsecurityChecker and HostCredentialLookup. Extracted registryHost() helper to eliminate duplicated URL-stripping. Added closure-held 30s TTL cache (sync.Mutex + map + time.Time) to both builders — eliminates per-OCI-pull DB queries. Threaded context.Context through all resolver call sites (was context.Background()). Updated ocivalidate/oci enricher option func signatures and insecureFor helpers. All tests updated and 2 cache-hit tests added.

title: Disambiguate overloaded 'Registry' naming across packages
description:
The word 'registry' is used for three unrelated concepts in the codebase, which makes main.go and surrounding wiring harder to read for newcomers:

1. `service.RegistryService` / `registrySvc` — CRUD over container/OCI registry configurations (zot, harbor, ghcr). User-facing domain object, DB-backed. Lives in internal/service/ and internal/api/types.go.
2. `extension.Registry` / `reg` — in-process plugin lifecycle manager (Init/Start/Stop). Lives in internal/extension/registry.go.
3. `enrichment.Registry` / `enrichReg` — in-process lookup map of enricher implementations by name. Lives in internal/enrichment/registry.go.

All three appear within ~10 lines of cmd/ocidex/main.go (lines 88-94), with only variable naming distinguishing them.

Proposal: rename the two non-domain 'Registry' types so 'Registry' unambiguously refers to the container-registry domain concept. Candidates:
- `extension.Registry` → `extension.Manager` or `extension.Host` (lifecycle coordinator)
- `enrichment.Registry` → `enrichment.Catalog` or `enrichment.Index` (name-keyed lookup)

Scope:
- Rename types and constructors
- Update call sites (main.go and any internal consumers)
- Update tests
- No behavior change

Acceptance:
- 'Registry' in code refers only to OCI/container registries
- `make check` passes
- Newcomer reading main.go can tell what each variable means without grep
notes:
Files: internal/extension/registry.go, internal/enrichment/registry.go, internal/enrichment/dispatcher.go, internal/extension/registry_test.go, internal/enrichment/dispatcher_test.go, cmd/ocidex/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go, cmd/scanner-worker/main.go, tests/scan_to_enrich_test.go
Approach: extension.Registry→extension.Manager (NewRegistry→NewManager); enrichment.Registry→enrichment.Catalog (NewRegistry→NewCatalog). Pure rename, no behavior change. make check passes (0 lint issues, all 14 packages ok, 63 frontend tests pass).

title: ocidex main requires GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/SECRET and SESSION_SECRET even in worker contexts
description:
The shared ocidex binary refuses to start without GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET, and SESSION_SECRET. Today this only matters because the worker Deployments accidentally run the API binary (see sibling bug on the Dockerfile), but once workers run their own binaries they shouldn't need OAuth config either.

Fix: gate the GITHUB_*/SESSION_SECRET requirement on whether the API server is actually being started. The worker entry points and the embedded-mode non-API paths don't authenticate browser sessions.

Surfaced during ocidex-pww (local Talos + Tilt dev loop) — had to inject these env vars into the dev overlay's worker patches as a workaround.
notes: Verified RESOLVED by binary split (701d7a6). Workers cmd/scanner-worker + cmd/enrichment-worker call config.Load() only; validateOAuthConfig is API-only (cmd/ocidex/main.go:61, migrate subcommand returns before run()). config.go does not mark GITHUB_*/SESSION_SECRET as required (only DATABASE_URL). Runtime check: both worker binaries built and run --once with GITHUB_*/SESSION_SECRET unset start cleanly and fail only at 'pinging database'. Added explicit regression case in internal/config/config_test.go ('OAuth/session vars not required (workers, ocidex-mf3)') so a future env:",required" tag fails a named test.

title: Tooltips on Show context / Show transitive toggles
description: DiffTreeView's Show-context and Show-transitive toggles need tooltips explaining what each does. 'Show context' = include unchanged ancestors of changed packages (helps see where a change lives in the tree). 'Show transitive' = include indirect dependencies (default off — only shows direct deps of the metadata.component). Without tooltips users have to guess.
acceptance_criteria: Hover or focus on each toggle reveals a brief tooltip. No regressions to default-off behavior.
notes: File: web/src/components/DiffTreeView.tsx. Added title attributes (native browser tooltips) to: 'Expand all changed' button, 'Show context' label (explains: include unchanged ancestors of changed packages; off by default), 'Show transitive' label (explains: include indirect deps; off by default — only direct deps shown). title: Spike: tree view animation and expand-all without browser crash
description:
## Why this exists

Two attempts to add smooth animation and expand-all to the dependency tree view (ocidex-0fp) both caused browser crashes. This spike should produce a concrete, validated approach before any implementation.

## What was tried

### Attempt 1 — always-mount all descendants
Pre-render the entire tree in the DOM (collapsed via CSS) so expand/collapse can animate without mount/unmount latency. Used CSS grid-template-rows: 0fr/1fr transition.

**Result:** Crashed immediately. A 500-package SBOM creates 500+ TreeNodeRow components each with createSignal + createMemo + createEffect + a recursive Set copy (nextVisited). The browser tab ran out of memory before painting anything.

### Attempt 2 — lazy-mount + cascade expand-all via createEffect(on(...))
Kept <Show when={expanded()}> for lazy mount. Added a SolidJS context carrying collapse/expand counter signals. Each TreeNodeRow ran createEffect(on(ctx.expand, ...)) without defer so newly-mounted children would also expand immediately — cascading depth-first until all nodes were expanded.

**Result:** Expand All still crashed. Rapidly mounting hundreds of components in a synchronous reactive cascade overwhelms the browser even with lazy mount. Collapse All worked fine (just sets expanded=false on mounted nodes).

## Root causes identified

1. **Reactive component count scales with O(visible nodes × depth)** — each TreeNodeRow allocates several reactive primitives; for large SBOMs even the lazy-mount approach can mount too many in one synchronous batch.

2. **nextVisited memo is O(depth) per node** — creates a new Set at every depth level, copying all ancestors. For a flat tree of 500 nodes each at depth 1 this is cheap, but real package DAGs can have shared nodes reached via long paths.

3. **Expand-all cascade is unbounded** — triggering a reactive signal that causes N mounts, each immediately running an effect that causes more mounts, is a synchronous avalanche.

## What to research

- **Centralized expansion state**: lift the Set<nodeRef> of expanded nodes to the tree root instead of per-node signals. Expand All becomes a single Set.add of all refs with no reactivity cascade. TreeNodeRow reads from this set reactively.

- **Virtualization**: render only the visible rows (windowed/virtual list). AG Grid, TanStack Virtual, or a simpler custom approach. Each row is a fixed-height div; only rows in the viewport are mounted.

- **Expand-all without cascade**: if expansion state is centralized, expand-all can be a single batch: compute all reachable non-cyclic refs, fill the Set, re-render. Children are still lazy but their "should expand" state is already in the Set when they mount.

- **Animation without always-mount**: CSS @starting-style (Chrome 117+, Firefox 129+) allows entry transitions even on first mount. Would let us use max-height or opacity transitions on lazy-mounted children without always-mounting.

- **Flatten then render**: instead of recursive component tree, flatten the visible rows into an array at the tree root (depth-first, filtered by expanded set), render with a single For loop over flat rows. This gives O(1) component overhead per node, enables windowing, and makes expand-all a pure data operation.

## Acceptance criteria

Chosen approach is validated in a local prototype (not necessarily the full implementation). Document: the approach, the library/pattern used (if any), and any trade-offs around animation vs. expand-all vs. virtualization.
notes:
Files: web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx
Approach: Ported the flat-DFS pattern already proven in DiffTreeView.tsx to DependencyTreeView. Deleted TreeNodeRow entirely. Centralized expansion state as createSignal(new Set<string>(), {equals:false}). Single visibleRows createMemo does DFS with a pathSet for cycle detection (replaces the nextVisited prop cascade). For loop renders flat rows — O(1) reactive primitives per node, no cascade. Added Expand All / Collapse All buttons; expand-all is one batch Set fill with no reactive cascade.
close_reason: Ported flat-DFS pattern from DiffTreeView to DependencyTreeView; TreeNodeRow deleted; expand-all is a single Set fill with no reactive cascade

title: Jobs tab: default filter to running/queued, add state and registry filter controls
description:
Admin > Jobs currently shows ALL jobs with no filtering UI, paginated but unfiltered. In normal operation the most interesting view is active jobs (running + queued). Completed and failed jobs are only relevant for debugging. Also, the ScanJobResponse has registry_id and repository fields that aren't used for filtering today.

**What needs to change:**

1. Default state filter: initialise the state filter signal to show running+queued jobs (pass state='running' by default to useListScanJobs). Note: the API only accepts a single state value — to show both running and queued, either fetch both in parallel and merge client-side, or default to 'running' only and let users select 'queued' separately. Prefer the two-request approach for accuracy; merge and sort by created_at desc.

2. Filter bar (above the table): add controls for:
   - State: segmented toggle or a <select> with options: All | Queued | Running | Succeeded | Failed (default: Running)
   - Search/filter by repository string (text input, client-side filter on job.repository and job.tag)
   - Registry: a <select> populated from useListRegistries(), filtering client-side by registry_id (since the API does not support this param today). Show 'All registries' as default.

3. Show job counts per state somewhere (can borrow from the scan_jobs stats already shown in the Status tab).

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — JobsTab function and useListScanJobs call
- web/src/api/queries/jobs.ts — may need to support parallel queries or rethink the accessor API
notes: Files: web/src/pages/admin/JobsTab.tsx\nApproach: Two parallel queries for active mode (running+queued merged client-side, sorted by created_at desc). Client-side repo text filter and registry_id filter. Registry dropdown populated from useListRegistries. Pagination hidden in active mode.

title: Registries: hide webhook-specific actions for poll-only registries
description:
In Admin > Registries, the table always shows 'Copy URL' and 'Regen Secret' buttons for every registry, even for types that do not support webhooks (docker and ghcr have webhook: false in TYPE_CAPS). This is confusing — those registries are poll-only and have no webhook URL or secret to manage.

**What needs to change:**
Conditionally render the webhook action buttons based on whether the registry actually uses webhooks. A registry uses webhooks when scan_mode is 'webhook' or 'both', AND when its type supports it (TYPE_CAPS[type].webhook === true).

The column/cell should:
- Still always render the Webhook URL column (to avoid layout shifts), but show a '-' or empty cell for poll-only registries
- Hide both 'Copy URL' and 'Regen Secret' buttons when the registry's effective scan_mode is 'poll' or the registry type has webhook: false

Condition to use (on each row):
  const hasWebhook = (reg) => reg.scan_mode !== 'poll' && TYPE_CAPS[reg.type as RegType]?.webhook === true

Wrap the two webhook buttons in a Show component using this predicate.

**Affected file:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — RegistriesTab table row, the 'Webhook URL' cell (lines ~824–843)
notes: Files: web/src/pages/admin/RegistriesTab.tsx Approach: Added hasWebhook(reg) predicate using TYPE_CAPS[type].webhook and scan_mode. Wrapped Copy URL + Regen Secret buttons in —>. The always renders to avoid layout shifts; only the button content is conditional.

title: Compare page: show architecture and build time in SBOM selection dropdowns
description:
The SBOM picker dropdowns on /diff currently use sbomLabel() which only shows 'version · date' or 'date · N components'. When an artifact has multiple SBOMs for the same version (e.g. multi-arch builds), the options are nearly indistinguishable. Architecture and build time are already present on SBOMSummary (fields: architecture and buildDate).

**What needs to change:**
Update the option labels in both the From and To SBOM selects in Diff.tsx to include architecture and build time when present.

Suggested label format:
- With version + arch + buildDate: '24.04 · amd64 · built Jan 15, 2025, 3:42 PM'
- With version + arch only: '24.04 · amd64 · ingested Jan 15, 2025'
- With version only (no arch): '24.04 · Jan 15, 2025'
- Fallback (no version): 'Jan 15, 2025, 3:42 PM · 342 components'

Implement a new sbomPickerLabel(sbom: SBOMSummary) helper in utils/format.ts that produces this richer label. Use it only in Diff.tsx option elements (not sbomLabel which is used in other contexts).

**Affected files:**
- web/src/utils/format.ts — add sbomPickerLabel()
- web/src/pages/Diff.tsx — use sbomPickerLabel() for option text
notes:

Approach: Added sbomPickerLabel() helper that includes architecture and buildDate when present. Uses existing formatDateTime() for build timestamps. Both From and To SBOM selects in Diff.tsx now use sbomPickerLabel() instead of sbomLabel(). sbomLabel() unchanged for other contexts.

title: Package view: filter plan/file-type components by default, with show toggle
description:
The flat PackagesTab already hard-filters components where type === 'file', but the DependencyTreeView does not — it renders every node from the raw dependency graph including build-plan entries (syft-detected .apkbuild, .spec, Nix .drv, and other file-type nodes). These inflate the tree and obscure the real package list.

**What needs to change:**
Apply a consistent plan-file filter across ALL views that display packages:
1. DependencyTreeView: filter out nodes from the dependency graph where the corresponding ComponentSummary has type === 'file' OR a purl type of 'file', before building the tree adjacency list.
2. PackagesTab (flat): already filters type === 'file'; no change needed for the filter logic itself, but expose the toggle to the user.
3. DiffEntry (compare/build-history): the 'Packages only' checkbox already filters to items with a purl, but plan files (type 'file') can still appear. Add a secondary filter that hides purl type === 'file' items when the toggle is on.

**UI:**
Add a 'Show plan files' toggle (default off) to:
- The filter bar in the package view (PackagesTab / tree view toolbar)
- The filter bar in DiffEntry (alongside the existing 'Packages only' checkbox)

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx
- web/src/components/DiffEntry.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactVersionHistory.tsx (passes props to DiffEntry)
- web/src/pages/Diff.tsx (passes props to DiffEntry)
notes:
Files: web/src/components/DiffTreeView.tsx (new), web/src/pages/ArtifactVersionHistory.tsx
Approach: Created DiffTreeView component that fetches the 'to' SBOM's dep graph and shows diff changes as an annotated dependency tree. Nodes with changes show a badge; unchanged ancestors of changed nodes show dimmed with child-count badge; tree auto-expands to first changed nodes. Removed packages not in the new graph appear as flat orphan rows. ArtifactVersionHistory now has a Tree/List toggle (default: Tree) that fetches the dep graph per BuildDiffEntry pair.

title: Package view: tree-first layout with rich node detail
description:
The SBOM detail page currently has two separate tabs — a flat table (Packages) and a basic tree (Dependency Tree). The tree shows only name + dep-count badge; the flat table has version, type badge, and PURL. Users lose all the useful column detail when switching to tree view.

**What needs to change:**
Collapse the two tabs into a single view. The primary layout should be the dependency tree (collapsed by default), but each node must carry the same details currently shown in the flat table: version, ecosystem/type badge, and PURL. A toggle in the toolbar should let users switch to the classic flat list.

**Specific design:**
- Tree mode (default): roots shown collapsed. Each row shows name (linked), version (mono), type badge, dep-count badge. Expanding a node reveals its direct deps as indented rows with the same detail columns.
- Flat mode: existing PackagesTab table, unchanged.
- Toggle: a small segmented control or icon button (Tree | List) in the filter bar.
- The DependencyTreeView component currently renders in a fixed 600px max-height div with no columns — replace that row-by-row approach with a proper table layout where the indent/expand toggle occupies the Name column.

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx — DependencyTreeView, PackagesTab, TreeNodeRow
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx — remove separate Dependencies tab, pass combined view

**Out of scope:** diff/compare pages (covered by a separate issue)
notes:
Files: web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx, web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx
Approach: Collapsed Packages+Dependencies tabs into a single view. PackagesTab now accepts optional depsGraph prop; when deps data with edges is available, Tree/List toggle appears in the filter bar and tree is shown by default (roots collapsed). DependencyTreeView rebuilt as a proper <table> with Name/Version/Type/Deps columns. TreeNodeRow renders <tr> elements with depth-based padding in the Name cell. Deps always eagerly loaded; tab signal and tab-bar removed from index.tsx.

notes: Files: Makefile (dev-cluster-up, dev-cluster-down, dev-up, dev-down targets)\nApproach: Implemented with different names as part of Talos+Tilt adoption. dev-cluster-up creates the Talos cluster+registry, dev-up runs Tilt. k8s-dev-logs not a named target but Tilt serves logs via its UI on :10350. Superseded by actual implementation.

title: 1.0.F.2 — k8s/base/secrets.yaml template
description: Add k8s/base/secrets.yaml template + .gitignore entry; document kustomize edit add secret workflow.
notes:
Files: k8s/base/secrets.yaml.example (new), .gitignore (added k8s/base/secrets.yaml entry)
Approach: Added a non-applied template Secret (`.example` suffix; not in kustomization.yaml) documenting the 5 keys consumed by k8s/base manifests via envFrom/secretKeyRef. Header runbook covers: per-key generation (openssl rand commands), DATABASE_URL composition, GitHub OAuth App setup with the exact `read:user` scope (from internal/service/auth.go:77), and the handoff path to the SOPS-encrypted Secret in the homelab repo (ocidex-0my.12). Placeholder values are REPLACE_ME_* so any accidental application visibly fails. Verified: kubectl kustomize k8s/base/ produces no Secret resource; git check-ignore confirms the .gitignore entry; all 5 keys match the consumers in k8s/base/*.yaml.

title: 1.0.E.3 — Document ephemeral job contract
description: Document the --once contract (env vars, exit codes) in docs/EPHEMERAL_JOBS.md. Operator (1.3) and CLI (1.2) consume this contract.
notes: Files: docs/EPHEMERAL_JOBS.md\nApproach: User-facing runbook with env var tables, usage examples, and a K8s Job YAML snippet for both scanner-worker and enrichment-worker.

title: Expose UNKNOWN severity filter on Vulnerabilities page
description: SEVERITY_TABS in web/src/pages/Vulnerabilities.tsx is All/CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW; UNKNOWN-severity vulns (common for fresh or distro advisories) are only reachable via All and easy to miss. Add an UNKNOWN tab — the backend ListTopVulnerabilities already filters by arbitrary severity string, so this is frontend-only. Run make frontend-lint-fix / frontend-lint.
acceptance_criteria: UNKNOWN tab filters the list to UNKNOWN-severity vulns; tab styling matches existing tabs.

title: CVSS v4 attack-profile chips
description: parseCvssVector (web/src/utils/vuln.ts) decodes only CVSS v3 metric tables; v4 vectors use different metrics (VC/VI/VA, SC/SI/SA, AT, no S) so v4 records render partial chips (only AV/AC/PR/UI). Add a v4 decode table selected by the CVSS:4.0 prefix, mapping v4 impact metrics to the same chip variants. Extend the existing tests if present, run make frontend-lint-fix then frontend-lint.
acceptance_criteria: A CVSS:4.0 vector renders a full chip set including impact metrics; v3 rendering unchanged.

title: Track checked-but-clean purls (purl_vuln_state)
description:
Purls with zero vulns have no package_vulnerability rows, so ListUnknownSBOMComponentPurls treats them as unknown forever: every SBOM ingest re-queries OSV for essentially all clean purls, and "never checked" is indistinguishable from "known clean".

Fix: add a lightweight purl_vuln_state (purl TEXT PRIMARY KEY, checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ) table written by ReplacePackageVulns/LookupPurls, and use it in the unknown-purl queries (and the scheduled-refresh unknown-purl union from ocidex-0le.7). Consider a staleness horizon so clean purls are still re-checked occasionally. Do after ocidex-0le.7 and ocidex-0le.8 land.
acceptance_criteria: Re-ingesting an SBOM whose purls were all checked (clean) triggers no OSV queries; unknown purls still do. Migration + queries + tests; make check passes.

title: Bound ingest-lookup concurrency in IngestVulnExtension
description: IngestVulnExtension.Init (internal/vuln/extension.go) spawns an unbounded goroutine per SBOMIngested event; a burst of ingests fans out parallel OSV batch queries with no cap. Add a small per-process semaphore/worker cap (e.g. 2 concurrent lookups) so bursts queue instead of stampeding OSV. Test that concurrent events serialize beyond the cap.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent SBOMIngested events never run more than the configured number of OSV lookups at once. make check passes.

value: epic-branch model: one branch per epic named after the epic ID (e.g. ocidex-hfi); work all child stories locally on that branch; do NOT push to remote mid-epic (CI cost); push only when the full epic is done and merged to main. Session end = commit, not push. The beads default session-close push step is superseded by this rule.
